title: Americans are so enamored of equality
grift: Americans are so enamored of equality
body:
## "Americans are so enamored of equality that they would rather be equal in slavery than unequal in freedom."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: Democracy extends the sphere of individual freedom
grift: Democracy extends the sphere of individual freedom
body:
## "Democracy extends the sphere of individual freedom, socialism restricts it. Democracy attaches all possible value to each man; socialism makes each man a mere agent, a mere number."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: Despotism may govern without faith
grift: Despotism may govern without faith
body:
## "Despotism may govern without faith, but liberty cannot."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: In democratic societies
grift: In democratic societies
body:
## "In democratic societies, each citizen is habitually engaged in the contemplation of a very puny object, namely, himself."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: Liberty cannot be established without morality
grift: Liberty cannot be established without morality
body:
## "Liberty cannot be established without morality, nor morality without faith."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: Society will develop a new kind of servitude
grift: Society will develop a new kind of servitude
body:
## "Society will develop a new kind of servitude which covers the surface of society with a network of complicated rules, through which the most original minds and the most energetic characters cannot penetrate."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: The American Republic will endure
grift: The American Republic will endure
body:
## "The American Republic will endure until the day Congress discovers that it can bribe the public with the public's money."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: The greatness of America
grift: The greatness of America
body:
## "The greatness of America lies not in being more enlightened than any other nation, but rather in her ability to repair her faults."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: The health of a democratic society
grift: The health of a democratic society
body:
## "The health of a democratic society may be measured by the quality of functions performed by private citizens."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: There are many men of principle
grift: There are many men of principle
body:
## "There are many men of principle in both parties in America, but there is no party of principle."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: A society that does not recognize
grift: A society that does not recognize
body:
## "A society that does not recognize that each individual has values of his own which he is entitled to follow can have no respect for the dignity of the individual and cannot really know freedom."

F.A. Hayek

title: Freedom is the will to be responsible to ourselves
grift: Freedom is the will to be responsible to ourselves
body:
## "Freedom is the will to be responsible to ourselves."

Friedrich Hayek

title: Hayek: The Fatal Conceit
grift: Hayek: The Fatal Conceit
body:
## "The Fatal Conceit: The curious task of economics is to demonstrate to men how little they really know about what they imagine they can design"

Friedrich Hayek

title: The freedom of the individual
grift: The freedom of the individual
body:
## "The freedom of the individual is the ultimate goal in a free society."

Friedrich Hayek

title: The more the state plans
grift: The more the state plans
body:
## "The more the state plans, the more difficult planning becomes for the individual."

Friedrich Hayek

title: The road to serfdom is paved with good intentions
grift: The road to serfdom is paved with good intentions
body:
## "The road to serfdom is paved with good intentions."

Friedrich Hayek

title: There is all the difference in the world
grift: There is all the difference in the world
body:
## "There is all the difference in the world between treating people equally and attempting to make them equal."

Friedrich Hayek

title: To be controlled in our economic pursuits
grift: To be controlled in our economic pursuits
body:
## "To be controlled in our economic pursuits means to be controlled in everything."

Friedrich Hayek

title: Liberty is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear
grift: Liberty is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear
body:
## "Liberty is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear."

George Orwell

title: Freedom is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear
grift: Freedom is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear
body:
## "Freedom is the right to tell people what they do not want to hear."

George Orwell

title: If liberty means anything at all
grift: If liberty means anything at all
body:
## "If liberty means anything at all, it means the right to tell people what they do not want to hear."

George Orwell

title: The real division is not between conservatives and revolutionaries
grift: The real division is not between conservatives and revolutionaries
body:
## "The real division is not between conservatives and revolutionaries but between authoritarians and libertarians."

George Orwell

title: Who controls the past controls the future
grift: Who controls the past controls the future
body:
## "Who controls the past controls the future. Who controls the present controls the past."

George Orwell

title: Economics is not about goods and services
grift: Economics is not about goods and services
body:
## "Economics is not about goods and services; it is about human choice and action."

Ludwig von Mises

title: Government cannot make man richer
grift: Government cannot make man richer
body:
## "Government cannot make man richer, but it can make him poorer."

Ludwig von Mises

title: Liberalism is not anarchism
grift: Liberalism is not anarchism
body:
## "Liberalism is not anarchism, nor has it anything whatsoever to do with anarchism. The liberal understands quite clearly that without resort to compulsion, the existence of society would be endangered and that behind the rules of conduct whose observance is necessary to assure peaceful human cooperation must stand the threat of force if the whole edifice of society is not to be continually at the mercy of any one of its members. One must be in a position to compel the person who will not respect the lives, health, personal freedom, or private property of others to acquiesce in the rules of life in society. This is the function that the liberal doctrine assigns to the state: the protection of property, liberty, and peace."

Ludwig von Mises

title: Repression by brute force is always
grift: Repression by brute force is always
body:
## "Repression by brute force is always a confession of the inability to make use of the better weapons of the intellect—better because they alone give promise of final success."

Ludwig von Mises

title: The issue is always the same: the government or
grift: The issue is always the same: the government or
body:
## "The issue is always the same: the government or the market. There is no third solution."

Ludwig von Mises

title: The problem is not to find better people
grift: The problem is not to find better people
body:
## "The problem is not to find better people to govern, but to allow a system that lets the governed govern themselves."

Ludwig von Mises

title: The program of classical liberalism = property
grift: The program of classical liberalism = property
body:
## "The program of classical liberalism, condensed into a single word, would have to read: property."

Ludwig von Mises

title: The worst evils which mankind has ever had to endure
grift: The worst evils which mankind has ever had to endure
body:
## "The worst evils which mankind has ever had to endure were inflicted by bad governments."

Ludwig von Mises

title: Whoever wishes to be free must
grift: Whoever wishes to be free must
body:
## "Whoever wishes to be free must make himself the master of his own passions."

Ludwig von Mises

title: A man's right to work
grift: A man's right to work
body:
## "A man's right to work as he will, to spend what he earns, to own property, to have the state as servant and not as master, they are essence of a tree economy, and on that freedom all our other freedoms depend."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Being powerful is like being a lady
grift: Being powerful is like being a lady
body:
## "Being powerful is like being a lady. If you have to tell people you are, you aren't."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Disciplining yourself to do what you know is right
grift: Disciplining yourself to do what you know is right
body:
## "Disciplining yourself to do what you know is right and important, although difficult, is the highroad to pride, self-esteem, and personal satisfaction."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Europe will never be like America
grift: Europe will never be like America
body:
## "Europe will never be like America. Europe is a product of history. America is a product of philosophy."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Free enterprise has enabled
grift: Free enterprise has enabled
body:
## "Free enterprise has enabled the creative and the acquisitive urges of man to be given expression in a way which benefits all members of society."

Margaret Thatcher

title: If they attack you personally
grift: If they attack you personally
body:
## "If they attack you personally it means they have not a single political argument left"

Margaret Thatcher

title: Let us never forget this fundamental truth
grift: Let us never forget this fundamental truth
body:
## "Let us never forget this fundamental truth: If the State wishes to spend more it can do so only by borrowing your savings or by taxing you more. It is no good thinking that someone else will pay - that 'someone else' is you."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Whenever we can, let us, like Luther, nail the truth to the door and let us do it in unambiguous English.
grift: Whenever we can, let us, like Luther, nail the truth to the door and let us do it in unambiguous English.
body:
## "Whenever we can, let us, like Luther, nail the truth to the door and let us do it in unambiguous English."

Margaret Thatcher

title: One way to destroy capitalism
grift: One way to destroy capitalism
body:
## "One way to destroy capitalism, said Lenin, was to devalue its currency. Another way is to debase its language."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Perhaps people do want more choice, do want to own their own homes, do want a greater say in their children's education.
grift: Perhaps people do want more choice, do want to own their own homes, do want a greater say in their children's education.
body:
## "Perhaps people do want more choice, do want to own their own homes, do want a greater say in their children's education."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Perhaps the pursuit of equality at any price does undermine liberty.
grift: Perhaps the pursuit of equality at any price does undermine liberty.
body:
## "Perhaps the pursuit of equality at any price does undermine liberty."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Socialism is like one of those horrible viruses
grift: Socialism is like one of those horrible viruses
body:
## "Socialism is like one of those horrible viruses: you no sooner discover a remedy for one version, than it spontaneously evolves into another."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Socialists say 'equality.' What they mean is 'levelling down'
grift: Socialists say 'equality.' What they mean is 'levelling down'
body:
## "Socialists say 'equality.' What they mean is 'levelling down'"

Margaret Thatcher

title: Socialists say 'Government aid'. What they mean is 'taxpayers' aid.
grift: Socialists say 'Government aid'. What they mean is 'taxpayers' aid.
body:
## "Socialists say 'Government aid'. What they mean is 'taxpayers' aid."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Socialists say 'Publicly owned'. What they mean is 'State controlled'
grift: Socialists say 'Publicly owned'. What they mean is 'State controlled'
body:
## "Socialists say 'Publicly owned'. What they mean is 'State controlled'"

Margaret Thatcher

title: Socialists say 'social justice'. What they mean is 'selective justice'.
grift: Socialists say 'social justice'. What they mean is 'selective justice'.
body:
## "Socialists say 'social justice'. What they mean is 'selective justice'."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Some socialists seem to believe
grift: Some socialists seem to believe
body:
## "Some socialists seem to believe that people should be numbers in a State computer. We believe they should be individuals. We are all unequal. No one, thank heavens, is like anyone else, however much the socialists may pretend otherwise. We believe that everyone has the right to be unequal but to us every human being is equally important."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Standing in the middle of the road is very dangerous
grift: Standing in the middle of the road is very dangerous
body:
## "Standing in the middle of the road is very dangerous; you get knocked down by traffic from both sides."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The best reply to full-blooded Socialism
grift: The best reply to full-blooded Socialism
body:
## "The best reply to full-blooded Socialism is not milk and water Socialism, it is genuine Conservatism."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The Left would rather the poor were poorer
grift: The Left would rather the poor were poorer
body:
## "The Left would rather the poor were poorer, provided the rich were less rich."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The problem with socialism
grift: The problem with socialism
body:
## "The problem with socialism is that you eventually run out of other people's money."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The rule of law is the most civilized concept
grift: The rule of law is the most civilized concept
body:
## "The rule of law is the most civilized concept humanity has yet devised."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The single biggest error during
grift: The single biggest error during
body:
## “The single biggest error during my political lifetime has been to confuse freedom with equality”

Margaret Thatcher

title: The Socialist myth
grift: The Socialist myth
body:
## "The Socialist myth is that governments can promise more and more, and take more and more, but that we can still keep our freedom. The truth is that if government does everything for you, it will take everything from you — first your money, then your dignity, and finally your freedom."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The state is not equipped to run businesses
grift: The state is not equipped to run businesses
body:
## "The state is not equipped to run businesses. It is equipped to run the law."

Margaret Thatcher

title: The trouble with socialism
grift: The trouble with socialism
body:
## "The trouble with socialism is that it always ends up running out of other people’s money."

Margaret Thatcher

title: Wealth is not created by government
grift: Wealth is not created by government
body:
## "Wealth is not created by government; it is created by the talent and enterprise of individuals in an atmosphere of freedom."

Margaret Thatcher

title: You can't make people good
grift: You can't make people good
body:
## "You can't make people good, kind, generous, thoughtful, or dutiful by compulsion"

Margaret Thatcher

title: If a private enterprise is a failure, it closes down
grift: If a private enterprise is a failure, it closes down
body:
## "If a private enterprise is a failure, it closes down - unless it can get a government subsidy to keep it going; if a government enterprise fails, it is expanded. I challenge you to find exceptions."

Milton Friedman

title: If you want to help the poor, prove it by getting rich yourself
grift: If you want to help the poor, prove it by getting rich yourself
body:
## "If you want to help the poor, demonstrate to them that they can become rich; prove it by getting rich yourself."

Milton Friedman

title: A society that puts equality before freedom
grift: A society that puts equality before freedom
body:
## "A society that puts equality before freedom will get neither. A society that puts freedom before equality will get a high degree of both."

Milton Friedman

title: After the fall of communism everybody
grift: After the fall of communism everybody
body:
## "After the fall of communism, everybody in the world agreed that Socialism was a failure. Everybody in the world, more or less, agreed that Capitalism was a success. And every Capitalist country in the world apparently deduced from that what the West needed was more Socialism."

Milton Friedman

title: Concentrated power is not rendered harmless
grift: Concentrated power is not rendered harmless
body:
## "Concentrated power is not rendered harmless by the good intentions of those who create it."

Milton Friedman

title: If you put the federal government in charge of the Sahara Desert
grift: If you put the federal government in charge of the Sahara Desert
body:
## "If you put the federal government in charge of the Sahara Desert, in five years there'd be a shortage of sand."

Milton Friedman

title: Inflation is taxation without legislation
grift: Inflation is taxation without legislation
body:
## "Inflation is taxation without legislation."

Milton Friedman

title: Nothing is more permanent than a temporary government measure
grift: Nothing is more permanent than a temporary government measure
body:
## "Nothing is more permanent than a temporary government measure."

Milton Friedman

title: Nothing is so permanent
grift: Nothing is so permanent
body:
## "Nothing is so permanent as a temporary government program."

Milton Friedman

title: The freedom to fail is vital
grift: The freedom to fail is vital
body:
## "The freedom to fail is vital if we are to have the freedom to succeed."

Milton Friedman

title: The government solution to a problem is usually as bad as the problem
grift: The government solution to a problem is usually as bad as the problem
body:
## "The government solution to a problem is usually as bad as the problem."

Milton Friedman

title: There is no such thing as a free lunch
grift: There is no such thing as a free lunch
body:
## "There is no such thing as a free lunch."

Milton Friedman

title: Underlying most arguments against the free market
grift: Underlying most arguments against the free market
body:
## "Underlying most arguments against the free market is a lack of belief in freedom itself."

Milton Friedman

title: All great change in America begins at the dinner table
grift: All great change in America begins at the dinner table
body:
## "All great change in America begins at the dinner table."

Ronald Reagan

title: As government expands, liberty contracts
grift: As government expands, liberty contracts
body:
## "As government expands, liberty contracts."

Ronald Reagan

title: Freedom is never more than one generation away from extinction
grift: Freedom is never more than one generation away from extinction
body:
## "Freedom is never more than one generation away from extinction."

Ronald Reagan

title: Government’s first duty is to protect the people
grift: Government’s first duty is to protect the people
body:
## "Government’s first duty is to protect the people, not run their lives."

Ronald Reagan

title: Government's first duty is to protect the people
grift: Government's first duty is to protect the people
body:
## "Government's first duty is to protect the people, is to protect the people, not run their lives."

Ronald Reagan

title: Government's view of the economy
grift: Government's view of the economy
body:
## "Government's view of the economy could be summed up in a few short phrases: If it moves, tax it. If it keeps moving, regulate it. And if it stops moving, subsidize it."

Ronald Reagan

title: History teaches that war begins
grift: History teaches that war begins
body:
## "History teaches that war begins when governments believe the price of aggression is cheap."

Ronald Reagan

title: Man is not free unless government is limited
grift: Man is not free unless government is limited
body:
## "Man is not free unless government is limited."

Ronald Reagan

title: No government ever voluntarily reduces itself in size
grift: No government ever voluntarily reduces itself in size
body:
## "No government ever voluntarily reduces itself in size. Government programs, once launched, never disappear."

Ronald Reagan

title: The future doesn’t belong to the fainthearted
grift: The future doesn’t belong to the fainthearted
body:
## "The future doesn’t belong to the fainthearted; it belongs to the brave."

Ronald Reagan

title: The nine most terrifying words in the English language are
grift: The nine most terrifying words in the English language are
body:
## "The nine most terrifying words in the English language are "I'm from the government, and I'm here to help."

Ronald Reagan

title: The problem is not that people are taxed too little
grift: The problem is not that people are taxed too little
body:
## "The problem is not that people are taxed too little, the problem is that government spends too much."

Ronald Reagan

title: The trouble with our liberal friends
grift: The trouble with our liberal friends
body:
## "The trouble with our liberal friends is not that they're ignorant it's just that they know so much that isn't so.'

Ronald Reagan

title: We are a nation that has a government
grift: We are a nation that has a government
body:
## "We are a nation that has a government - not the other way around."

Ronald Reagan

title: We must reject the idea
grift: We must reject the idea
body:
## "We must reject the idea that every time a law's broken, society is guilty rather than the lawreaker. It is time to restore the American precept that each individual is accountable for his actions."

Ronald Reagan

title: You can’t be for big government
grift: You can’t be for big government
body:
## "You can’t be for big government, big taxes, and big bureaucracy and still be for the little guy."

Ronald Reagan

title: A pro-black stance by white intellectuals
grift: A pro-black stance by white intellectuals
body:
## "A pro-black stance by white intellectuals enhances the latter's moral standing and self-esteem, whether or not the particular manifestation of that stance helps or harms blacks on net balance."

Thomas Sowell, *Black Rednecks and White Liberals (2005)*

title: Activism is a way for useless people to feel important
grift: Activism is a way for useless people to feel important
body:
## "Activism is a way for useless people to feel important, even if the consequences of their activism are counterproductive for those they claim to be helping and damaging to the fabric of society as a whole."

Thomas Sowell, *Ever Wonder Why? And Other Controversial Essays (2006)*

title: Affirmative action is racism under new
grift: Affirmative action is racism under new
body:
## "Affirmative action is racism under new management."

Thomas Sowell, *Barbarians Inside the Gates and Other Controversial Essays (1999)*

title: Although the big word on the
grift: Although the big word on the
body:
## "Although the big word on the left is 'compassion,' the big agenda on the left is dependency."

Thomas Sowell, *Depending on Dependency (2012)*

title: Among the many other questions raised
grift: Among the many other questions raised
body:
## "Among the many other questions raised by the nebulous concept of “greed” is why it is a term applied almost exclusively to those who want to earn more money or to keep what they have already earned—never to those wanting to take other people’s money in taxes or to those wishing to live on the largesse dispensed from such taxation. No amount of taxation is ever described as “greed” on the part of government or the clientele of government."

Thomas Sowell

title: Among the people appointed as czars
grift: Among the people appointed as czars
body:
## "Among the people appointed as czars by President Obama have been people who have praised enemy dictators like Mao, or who have seen the public schools as places to promote sexual practices contrary to the values of most Americans, to a captive audience of children.

Those who say that the Obama administration should have investigated those people more thoroughly before appointing them are missing the point completely. Why should we assume that Barack Obama didn’t know what such people were like, when he has been associating with precisely these kinds of people for decades before he reached the White House?

Nothing is more consistent with his lifelong patterns than putting such people in government—people who reject American values, resent Americans in general and successful Americans in particular, as well as resenting America’s influence in the world."

Thomas Sowell

title: Any politician who can be elected
grift: Any politician who can be elected
body:
## "Any politician who can be elected only by turning Americans against other Americans is too dangerous to be elected."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: Anyone who wants reparations based on
grift: Anyone who wants reparations based on
body:
## "Anyone who wants reparations based on history will have to gerrymander history very carefully. Otherwise, practically everybody would owe reparations to practically everybody else."

Thomas Sowell, *The Benfits And Hypocrisy Of Playing The Slavery Card (1995)*

title: As a young Marxist in college
grift: As a young Marxist in college
body:
## "As a young Marxist in college during the 1950s heyday of the anti-Communist crusade led by Sen. Joseph McCarthy, I had more freedom to express my views in class, without fear of retaliation, than conservative students have on many campuses today."

Thomas Sowell, *Dry Rot in Academia (2016)*

title: As imposed wage rates rise, so
grift: As imposed wage rates rise, so
body:
## "As imposed wage rates rise, so do job qualifications, so that less skilled or less experienced workers become 'unemployable.' 

Think about it. Every one of us would be 'unemployable' if our pay rates were raised high enough."

Thomas Sowell, *Ever Wonder Why? And Other Controversial Essays (2006)*

title: As of 1948, during this period
grift: As of 1948, during this period
body:
## "As of 1948, during this period of no effective minimum wage law, the unemployment rates of both black and white teenagers were just a fraction of what they would become in later years, as minimum wage rates began rising in the 1950s to catch up, and then keep up, with inflation in later years."

Thomas Sowell, *Discrimination and Disparities (2018)*

title: Ask ten people what fairness means
grift: Ask ten people what fairness means
body:
## "Ask ten people what ‘fairness’ means and you can get eleven different definitions. Expecting the government to promote ‘fairness’ is just giving politicians more arbitrary power."

Thomas Sowell, *Ever Wonder Why? And Other Controversial Essays (2006), § Random Thoughts*

title: Barack Obama has not only said
grift: Barack Obama has not only said
body:
## "Barack Obama has not only said that he is out to “change the United States of America,” the people he has been associated with for years have expressed in words and deeds their hostility to the values, the principles and the people of this country."

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010), p. 6*

title: Barack Obamas political genius is his
grift: Barack Obamas political genius is his
body:
## "Barack Obama’s political genius is his ability to say things that will sound good to people who have not followed the issues in any detail, regardless of how obviously fraudulent what he says may be to those who have."

Thomas Sowell, *Obama’s ‘Balanced’ Approach (2011)*

title: Because of the neglect of history
grift: Because of the neglect of history
body:
## "Because of the neglect of history in our educational system, most people have no idea how many of the great American fortunes were created by people who were born and raised in worse poverty than the average welfare-recipient today."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002), p. 302*

title: Blacks were not enslaved because they were black
grift: Blacks were not enslaved because they were black
body:
## "Blacks were not enslaved because they were black but because they were available. Slavery has existed in the world for thousands of years. Whites enslaved other whites in Europe for centuries before the first black was brought to the Western hemisphere. Asians enslaved Europeans. Asians enslaved other Asians. Africans enslaved other Africans, and indeed even today in North Africa, blacks continue to enslave blacks."

Thomas Sowell, *The Benefits and Hypocrisy of Playing the Slavery Card (1995)*

title: Blaming others for anything in which
grift: Blaming others for anything in which
body:
## "Blaming others for anything in which blacks lag has become standard operating procedure among white liberals. If blacks do not pass bar exams or medical board tests as often as whites or Asians, then that shows that something is wrong with those tests, as far as many white liberals are concerned."

Thomas Sowell, *Black Rednecks and White Liberals (2005), p. 52-53*

title: Both free speech rights and property rights
grift: Both free speech rights and property rights
body:
## "Both free speech rights and property rights belong legally to individuals, but their real function is social, to benefit vast numbers of people who do not themselves exercise these."

Thomas Sowell

title: Both the Sicilian mafia and the
grift: Both the Sicilian mafia and the
body:
## "Both the Sicilian mafia and the criminal tongs in China began as movements to defend the oppressed, so perhaps we should not be so painfully surprised that venerable American civil rights organizations have begun to degenerate into extortion rackets."

Thomas Sowell, *Ever Wonder Why? And Other Controversial Essays (2006), p. 450*

title: But life does not ask us
grift: But life does not ask us
body:
## "But life does not ask us what we want. It presents us with options. Economics is one of the ways of trying to make the most of those options."

Thomas Sowell, *Basic Economics (2000)*

title: By cheering on counterproductive attitudes, making
grift: By cheering on counterproductive attitudes, making
body:
## "By cheering on counterproductive attitudes, making excuses for self-defeating behavior, and promoting the belief that “racism” accounts for most of blacks’ problems, white intellectuals serve their own psychic, ideological, and political interests.They are the kinds of friends who can do more harm than enemies.

A crucial fact about white liberals must be kept in mind:They are not simply in favor of blacks in general. Their solicitude is poured out for blacks as victims, blacks as welfare mothers, criminals, political activists against the larger society, as well as those blacks who serve as general counter-cultural symbols against the larger society. 

White liberals have nothing approaching the same interest in blacks as the principal victims of black criminals or as people advancing themselves within the existing framework of American society, including many who have risen within the military, nor do they get particularly worked up over blacks who build up their own human capital or business capital. 

None of the many reports of black schools that excel academically seems to arouse any great interest among white liberals. It was not the liberals in Washington, but the Reagan administration, which offered successful black educator Marva Collins an appointment as Secretary of Education."

Thomas Sowell, *Black Rednecks and White Liberals (2005), p. 57-58*

title: Competition does a much more effective
grift: Competition does a much more effective
body:
## "Competition does a much more effective job than government at protecting consumers."

Thomas Sowell, *Compassion Versus Guilt and other Essays (1985)*

title: Continuing transactions between buyer and seller
grift: Continuing transactions between buyer and seller
body:
## "Continuing transactions between buyer and seller make sense only if value is subjective, each getting what is worth more subjectively. Economic transactions are not a zero-sum process, where one person loses whatever the other person gains."

Thomas Sowell, *Basic Economics (2000)*

title: Criminals understand that they are at
grift: Criminals understand that they are at
body:
## "Criminals understand that they are at war with society. But many of those whose jobs it is to protect society do not want to treat criminals like mortal enemies.

Somewhere out there in the affluent suburbs, there is a white liberal judge who feels good about himself because he gave a ghetto youth another chance.

The price of his moral vanity is paid in blood — by others."

Thomas Sowell

title: Degrees show that you have knowledge
grift: Degrees show that you have knowledge
body:
## "Degrees show that you have knowledge in some special area. Too often they embolden people to pontificate on a wide range of other subjects where they don’t know what they are talking about."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011)*

title: Different people have very different reactions
grift: Different people have very different reactions
body:
## "Different people have very different reactions to President Barack Obama. Those who listen to his rhetoric are often inspired, while those who follow what he actually does are often appalled."

Thomas Sowell, *Obama rhetoric is fine, results lagging (2009)*

title: Does anyone seriously believe that excuse-making
grift: Does anyone seriously believe that excuse-making
body:
## "Does anyone seriously believe that excuse-making and counterproductive policies on crime are a favor to black communities? It is now so widely known and so well documented that black people are the main victims of black criminals that there is no room for honest misunderstanding on this subject."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § PASSING IN REVIEW*

title: Education is not merely neglected in
grift: Education is not merely neglected in
body:
## "Education is not merely neglected in many of our schools today, but is replaced to a great extent by ideological indoctrination."

Thomas Sowell, *Why's There So Little Free Speech on Campus? (2013)*

title: Elections should be held on April
grift: Elections should be held on April
body:
## "Elections should be held on April 16th- the day after we pay our income taxes. That is one of the few things that might discourage politicians from being big spenders."

Thomas Sowell, *Ever Wonder Why? And Other Controversial Essays (2006)*

title: Entitlement is not only the opposite
grift: Entitlement is not only the opposite
body:
## "Entitlement" is not only the opposite of achievement, it undermines incentives to do all the hard work that leads to achievement."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: Envy plus rhetoric equals 'social justice'.
grift: Envy plus rhetoric equals 'social justice'.
body:
## "Envy plus rhetoric equals 'social justice'."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011)*

title: Envy used to be just a
grift: Envy used to be just a
body:
## "Envy used to be just a human failing, but today it is a major industry. Politicians, journalists and academics are all part of that industry, which some call ‘social justice.’"

Thomas Sowell, *Hard Times for Envy (2003)*

title: Envy was once considered to be
grift: Envy was once considered to be
body:
## "Envy was once considered to be one of the seven deadly sins before it became one of the most admired virtues under its new name, 'social justice'."

Thomas Sowell, *The Quest for Cosmic Justice (1999)*

title: 'Equality' is one of the crucial
grift: 'Equality' is one of the crucial
body:
## "'Equality' is one of the crucial far-fetched ideas of our time. From the soapbox demagogue to the highest court in the land, statistical disparities in outcomes have been treated as virtual proof that somebody was treated unfairly. 

Apparently, there is no way that they could have performed differently, because of the implicit assumption of equality."

Thomas Sowell, *Barbarians Inside the Gates and Other Controversial Essays (1999)*

title: Equality of rights does not mean
grift: Equality of rights does not mean
body:
## "Equality of rights does not mean equality of results. I can have all the equal treatment in the world on a golf course and I will not finish within shouting distance of Tiger Woods."

Thomas Sowell, _Affirmative Action and Gay Marriage (2008)_

title: Europe is belatedly discovering
grift: Europe is belatedly discovering
body:
## "Europe is belatedly discovering how unbelievably stupid it was to import millions of people from cultures that despise Western values and which often promote hatred toward the people who have let them in."

Thomas Sowell, *Anti-Trust Law and Lawlessness (2015)*

title: Everyone hated the idea of being
grift: Everyone hated the idea of being
body:
## "Everyone hated the idea of being a slave but few had any qualms about enslaving others. Slavery was just not an issue, not even among intellectuals, much less among political leaders, until the 18th century – and then it was an issue only in Western civilization. Among those who turned against slavery in the 18th century were George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, Patrick Henry and other American leaders. You could research all of the 18th century Africa or Asia or the Middle East without finding any comparable rejection of slavery there. But who is singled out for scathing criticism today? American leaders of the 18th century."

Thomas Sowell

title: Evidence and compelling logic is an
grift: Evidence and compelling logic is an
body:
## "Evidence and compelling logic is an enemy of dogmas, and one dealt with as an enemy by small but growing numbers of modern intellectuals."

Thomas Sowell, *Intellectuals and Society (2009)*

title: Facts are seldom allowed to contaminate
grift: Facts are seldom allowed to contaminate
body:
## "Facts are seldom allowed to contaminate the beautiful vision of the left. What matters to the true believers are the ringing slogans, endlessly repeated. Darwinian adaptation to environment applies not only to nature but also to society. 

Just as you don’t find eagles living in the ocean or fish living on mountain tops, so you don’t find leftists concentrated where their ideas have to stand the test of performance."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011)*

title: Freedom has cost too much blood
grift: Freedom has cost too much blood
body:
## "Freedom has cost too much blood and agony to be relinquished at the cheap price of rhetoric."

Thomas Sowell, *Knowledge and Decisions (1980)*

title: Freedom is unlikely to be lost
grift: Freedom is unlikely to be lost
body:
## "Freedom is unlikely to be lost all at once and openly. It is far more likely to be eroded away, bit by bit, amid glittering promises and expressions of noble."

Thomas Sowell, *The Quest for Cosmic Justice (1999), p. 184*

title: Freedom means nothing if it does
grift: Freedom means nothing if it does
body:
## "Freedom means nothing if it does not mean the freedom to do what other people don't like. Everyone was free to be a Communist under the Stalin dictatorship, and everyone is free to be a Muslim in Saudi Arabia. Yet whole generations are coming out of our colleges where only those who are politically correct are free to speak their minds. What kind of America will they create?"

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: Guilt has so furtively stolen into many hearts
grift: Guilt has so furtively stolen into many hearts
body:
## "Guilt has so furtively stolen into many hearts and minds that people feel apologetic about being civilized, educated, and productive when others are barbaric, uneducated, and parasitic. When civilization apologizes to barbarism, something has gone very wrong at a very fundamental level."

Thomas Sowell

title: Have you ever heard anyone as
grift: Have you ever heard anyone as
body:
## "Have you ever heard anyone as incoherent as the people staging protests across the country? Taxpayers ought to be protesting against having their money spent to educate people who end up unable to say anything beyond repeating political catch phrases."

Thomas Sowell, *Random thoughts on the passing scene (2011)*

title: High tax rates in the upper
grift: High tax rates in the upper
body:
## "High tax rates in the upper income brackets allow politicians to win votes with class warfare rhetoric, painting their opponents as defenders of the rich. Meanwhile, the same politicians can win donations from the rich by creating tax loopholes that can keep the rich from actually paying those higher tax rates - or perhaps any taxes at all. What is worse than class warfare is phony class warfare. Slippery talk about 'fairness' is at the heart of this fraud by politicians seeking to squander more of the nation's resources."

Thomas Sowell, *The 'Fairness' Fraud (2012)*

title: Historians of the future, when they
grift: Historians of the future, when they
body:
## "Historians of the future, when they look back on our times, may be completely baffled when trying to understand how Western civilization welcomed vast numbers of people hostile to the fundamental values of Western civilization, people who had been taught that they have a right to kill those who do not share their beliefs."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: Historians of the future will have
grift: Historians of the future will have
body:
## "Historians of the future will have a hard time figuring out how so many organized groups of strident jackasses succeeded in leading us around by the nose and morally intimidating the majority into silence."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), p. 399*

title: How are children supposed to learn
grift: How are children supposed to learn
body:
## "How are children supposed to learn to act like adults, when so much of what they see on television shows adults acting like children?"

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: How can anyone consider it to
grift: How can anyone consider it to
body:
## "How can anyone consider it to be either logical or moral to force other people to be defenseless because of a theory without any factual evidence? Yet that is what gun control laws amount to."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: Human beings are going to make mistakes
grift: Human beings are going to make mistakes
body:
## "Human beings are going to make mistakes, whether in the market or in the government. The difference is that survival in the market requires recognizing mistakes and changing course before you go bankrupt. But survival in politics requires denying mistakes and sticking with the policies you advocated, while blaming others for the bad results."

Thomas Sowell, *Basic Economics (2000)*

title: I don't want people making decisions
grift: I don't want people making decisions
body:
## "I don't want people making decisions who don't pay the price of their decisions, and that's what politics is all about."

Thomas Sowell, *C-SPAN Interview*

title: I have never understood why it is 'greed'
grift: I have never understood why it is 'greed'
body:
## "I have never understood why it is 'greed' to want to keep the money you've earned, but not greed to want to take somebody else's money."

Thomas Sowell, *Barbarians Inside the Gates and Other Controversial Essays (1999)*

title: If it were possible to enforce
grift: If it were possible to enforce
body:
## "If it were possible to enforce a ban on lying, a ghastly silence would fall over the city of Washington."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011)*

title: If politicians stopped meddling with things
grift: If politicians stopped meddling with things
body:
## "If politicians stopped meddling with things they don’t understand, there would be a more drastic reduction in the size of government than anyone in either party advocates."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § Random Thoughts*

title: If there is not equality of
grift: If there is not equality of
body:
## "If there is not equality of outcomes among people born to the same parents and raised under the same roof, why should equality of outcomes be expected—or assumed—when conditions are not nearly so comparable?"

Thomas Sowell

title: If there is one thing that
grift: If there is one thing that
body:
## "If there is one thing that is bipartisan in Washington, it is brazen hypocrisy."

Thomas Sowell, *Supreme Hypocrisy (2016)*

title: If you are not prepared to use force
grift: If you are not prepared to use force
body:
## "If you are not prepared to use force to defend civilization, then be prepared to accept barbarism."

Thomas Sowell

title: If you cannot achieve equality
grift: If you cannot achieve equality
body:
## "If you cannot achieve equality of performance among people born to the same parents and raised under the same roof, how realistic is it to expect to achieve it across broader and deeper social divisions?"

Thomas Sowell

title: If you dont believe in the
grift: If you dont believe in the
body:
## "If you don’t believe in the innate unreasonableness of human beings, just try raising children."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002), p. 321*

title: If you have always believed that
grift: If you have always believed that
body:
## "If you have always believed that everyone should play by the same rules and be judged by the same standards, that would have gotten you labeled a radical 50 years ago, a liberal 25 years ago and a racist today."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: If you want more of something, subsidize it
grift: If you want more of something, subsidize it
body:
## "If you want more of something, subsidize it; if you want less of something, tax it."

Thomas Sowell

title: If you want to fulfill your
grift: If you want to fulfill your
body:
## "If you want to fulfill your duty as a citizen, then you need to become an informed voter. And if you are not informed, then the most patriotic thing you can do on election day is stay home. Otherwise your vote, based on whims or emotions, is playing Russian roulette with the fate of this nation."

Thomas Sowell, *Why Have Elections? (2015)*

title: If you want to see the poor remain poor
grift: If you want to see the poor remain poor
body:
## "If you want to see the poor remain poor, generation after generation, just keep the standards low in their schools and make excuses for their academic shortcomings and personal misbehavior. But please don't congratulate yourself on your compassion."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002), p. 306*

title: If youre going to have reparations
grift: If youre going to have reparations
body:
## "If you’re going to have reparations for slavery, it’s going to be the greatest transfer of wealth back and forth, because the number of Whites who were enslaved in North Africa by the Barbary Pirates exceeded the number of Africans enslaved in the United States."

Thomas Sowell, *Maverick: Jason Riley On The Life And Times Of Thomas Sowell (2020)*

title: I'm so old that I can
grift: I'm so old that I can
body:
## "I'm so old that I can remember when most of the people promoting race hate were white."

Thomas Sowell, *Who Is Racist? (2013)*

title: Immigration laws are the only laws
grift: Immigration laws are the only laws
body:
## "Immigration laws are the only laws that are discussed in terms of how to help people who break them."

Thomas Sowell

title: In an increasingly global economy, Europeans
grift: In an increasingly global economy, Europeans
body:
## "In an increasingly global economy, Europeans and Americans might be very hesitant to buy telecommunications equipment made halfway around the world in South Korea. But, once the Samsung brand acquired a track record, people in Berlin or Chicago would just as soon buy Samsung products as buy competing products manufactured down the street. Asian companies in general have only in relatively recent history begun investing much time and money in making their brand names widely known, and still they spend less on this than other multinational companies. However, brand names like Toyota, Honda, and Nikon are recognized around the world, and the Cathay Pacific airline and the Shangri-La hotel chain are also becoming better known internationally.

Brand names are not guarantees. But they do reduce the range of uncertainty. If a hotel sign says Ritz-Carlton, chances are you will not have to worry about whether the bed sheets in your room were changed since the last person slept there. Even if you stop at a dingy and run-down little store in a strange town, you are not afraid to drink a soda they sell you, if it is a bottle or can of Coca-Cola or Seven Up. Imagine, however, if the owner of this unsavory little place mixed you a soda at his own soda fountain. Would you have the same confidence in drinking it?"

Thomas Sowell, *Basic Economics (2000), p. 1043-1044*

title: In other words, when people choose
grift: In other words, when people choose
body:
## "In other words, when people choose their occupations according to what the public wants and is willing to pay for. that is ‘greed,’ but when the public is forced to pay for what the anointed want done, that is ‘public service.’"

Thomas Sowell, *The Vision of the Anointed: Self-Congratulation as a Basis for Social Policy (1995), Ch. 7*

title: In the academic world, diversity means
grift: In the academic world, diversity means
body:
## "In the academic world, diversity means black leftists, white leftists, female leftists, and Hispanic leftists. Demographic diversity conceals ideological conformity."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: In the United States, the group
grift: In the United States, the group
body:
## "In the United States, the group hardest hit by minimum wage laws are black male teenagers. Those who refuse to admit that the minimum wage is the reason for high unemployment rates among young blacks blame racism, lack of education and whatever else occurs to them."

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010)*

title: 'Income distribution'
grift: 'Income distribution'
body:
## "Despite a voluminous and often fervent literature on 'income distribution', the cold fact is that most income is not distributed: It is earned."

Thomas Sowell, *The Vision of the Anointed: Self-Congratulation as a Basis for Social Policy (1995)*

title: Increasing numbers of educators and the
grift: Increasing numbers of educators and the
body:
## "Increasing numbers of educators and the intelligentsia seem to have devoted themselves to undermining or destroying a sense of personal responsibility and making society responsible instead."

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010)*

title: Intellectuals as a class are dependent
grift: Intellectuals as a class are dependent
body:
## "Intellectuals as a class are dependent upon the backing of political power to impose their visions on the underlying population."

Thomas Sowell, *Knowledge and Decisions (1980)*

title: Intellectuals give people who have the
grift: Intellectuals give people who have the
body:
## "Intellectuals give people who have the handicap of poverty the further handicap of a sense of victimhood."

Thomas Sowell, *Intellectuals and Society (2009)*

title: Intolerance may not promote progress but
grift: Intolerance may not promote progress but
body:
## "Intolerance may not promote progress but it can promote survival. An intolerant Islamic world may outlast the Western world that seems ready to tolerate anything, including the undermining of its own fundamental values and threats to its continued existence."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: Is diversity our strength? Or anybodys
grift: Is diversity our strength? Or anybodys
body:
## "Is diversity our strength? Or anybody’s strength, anywhere in the world? Does Japan's homogeneous population cause the Japanese to suffer? Have the Balkans been blessed by their heterogeneity - or does the very word 'Balkanization' remind us of centuries of strife, bloodshed and unspeakable atrocities, extending into our own times?"

Thomas Sowell

title: It is a classic notion on
grift: It is a classic notion on
body:
## "It is a classic notion on the left in general, and of environmentalist zealots in particular, that no one can disagree with them unless they are either uninformed or dishonest."

Thomas Sowell, *Global Hot Air: Part II (2007)*

title: It is amazing how many people
grift: It is amazing how many people
body:
## "It is amazing how many people think that they can answer an argument by attributing bad motives to those who disagree with them."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: It is amazing how much panic
grift: It is amazing how much panic
body:
## "It is amazing how much panic one honest man can spread among a multitude of hypocrites."

Thomas Sowell, *Is Reality Optional (1993)*

title: It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions
grift: It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions
body:
## "It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions than by putting those decisions in the hands of people who pay no price for being wrong."

Thomas Sowell, *Wake up, Parents (2000)*

title: It is not enough for us
grift: It is not enough for us
body:
## "It is not enough for us to discover belatedly that we have been lied to. It is far more important that we remember who the liars are and not be so gullible next time."

Thomas Sowell, *Barbarians Inside the Gates and Other Controversial Essays (1999)*

title: It is so easy to be
grift: It is so easy to be
body:
## "It is so easy to be wrong-and to persist in being wrong-when the costs of being wrong are paid by others."

Thomas Sowell, *The Vision of the Anointed: Self-Congratulation as a Basis for Social Policy (1995), p. 136*

title: It is usually futile to try to talk
grift: It is usually futile to try to talk
body:
## "It is usually futile to try to talk facts and analysis to people who are enjoying a sense of moral superiority in their ignorance."

Thomas Sowell

title: It takes considerable knowledge just to
grift: It takes considerable knowledge just to
body:
## "It takes considerable knowledge just to realize the extent of your own ignorance."

Thomas Sowell, *A Childish Letter (1998)*

title: It takes no more research than
grift: It takes no more research than
body:
## "It takes no more research than a trip to almost any public library or college to show the incredibly lopsided coverage of slavery in the United States or in the Western Hemisphere, as compared to the meager writings on even larger number of Africans enslaved in the Islamic countries of the Middle East and North Africa, not to mention the vast numbers of Europeans also enslaved in centuries past in the Islamic world and within Europe itself. 

At least a million Europeans were enslaved by North African pirates alone from 1500 to 1800, and some European slaves were still being sold on the auction blocks in the Egypt, years after the Emancipation Proclamation freed blacks in the United States."

Thomas Sowell, *Black Rednecks and White Liberals (2005)*

title: It was the Africans who enslaved
grift: It was the Africans who enslaved
body:
## "It was the Africans who enslaved their fellow Africans, selling some of these slaves to Europeans or to Arabs and keeping others for themselves. Even at the peak of the Atlantic slave trade, Africans retained more slaves for themselves than they sent to the Western Hemisphere."

Thomas Sowell, *Black Rednecks and White Liberals (2005)*

title: It was Thomas Edison who brought
grift: It was Thomas Edison who brought
body:
## "It was Thomas Edison who brought us electricity not the Sierra Club. It was the Wright Brothers who got us off the ground not the Federal Aviation Administration.

## It was Henry Ford who ended the isolation of millions of Americans by making the automobile affordable, not Ralph Nader.

## Those who have helped the poor the most have not been those who have gone around loudly expressing compassion for the poor but those who found ways to make industry more productive and distribution more efficient so that the poor of today can afford things that the affluent of yesterday could only dream about."

Thomas Sowell, *The Real Public Service (2010)*

title: It's amazing how fast people learn
grift: It's amazing how fast people learn
body:
## "It's amazing how fast people learn when they are not insulated from the consequences of their decisions."

Thomas Sowell

title: It is fascinating to watch politicians
grift: It is fascinating to watch politicians
body:
## "It is fascinating to watch politicians come up with 'solutions' to problems that are a direct result of their previous solutions. In many cases the most efficient thing to do would be to repeal their previous solution and stop being so gung-ho for creating new solutions."

Thomas Sowell

title: I've often said there are three
grift: I've often said there are three
body:
## "I've often said there are three questions that would destroy most of the arguments on the left. The first is 'compared to what?' The second is 'at what cost?' And the third is 'what hard evidence do you have?'"

Thomas Sowell, *Thomas Sowell: In The Right Direction (2005)*

title: Journalists cannot serve two masters. To
grift: Journalists cannot serve two masters. To
body:
## "Journalists cannot serve two masters. To the extent that they take on the task of suppressing information or biting their tongue for the sake of some political agenda, they are betraying the trust of the public and corrupting their own profession."

Thomas Sowell, *The Media and Politics (2008)*

title: Knowledge can be enormously costly, and
grift: Knowledge can be enormously costly, and
body:
## "Knowledge can be enormously costly, and is often scattered in widely uneven fragments, too small to be individually usable in decision making. The communication and coordination of these scattered fragments of knowledge is one of the basic problems- perhaps the basic problem- of any society."

Thomas Sowell, *Knowledge and Decisions (1980)*

title: Let the media discover that any
grift: Let the media discover that any
body:
## "Let the media discover that any of the sins that have plagued the human race for thousands of years still exist in the United States, and it becomes a special condemnation of American society. The morally anointed are constantly outraged that we have not obliterated racism, poverty, or disease. It would be fascinating to know who has—and how. But a look around the world shows that our problems in these areas are like a sprained ankle compared to cancer."

Thomas Sowell

title: Liberalism is totalitarianism with a human face.
grift: Liberalism is totalitarianism with a human face.
body:
## "Liberalism is totalitarianism with a human face."

Thomas Sowell

title: Liberals take positions that make them
grift: Liberals take positions that make them
body:
## "Liberals take positions that make them look good and feel good — and show very little interest in the actual consequences for others, even when liberal policies are leaving havoc in their wake."

Thomas Sowell

title: Liberals try to show their concern
grift: Liberals try to show their concern
body:
## "Liberals try to show their concern for the poor by raising the level of minimum wage laws. Yet they show no interest in hard evidence that minimum wage laws create disastrous levels of unemployment among young blacks in this country."

Thomas Sowell, *Liberalism Versus Blacks (2013)*

title: Life does not ask us what
grift: Life does not ask us what
body:
## "Life does not ask us what we want. It presents us with options."

Thomas Sowell, *Basic Economics (2000), p. 23*

title: Many colleges claim that they develop
grift: Many colleges claim that they develop
body:
## "Many colleges claim that they develop 'leaders.' All too often, that means turning out graduates who cannot feel fulfilled unless they are telling other people what to do. There are already too many people like that, and they are a menace to everyone else's freedom."

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010), p. 335*

title: Many colleges claim that they develop
grift: Many colleges claim that they develop
body:
## "Many colleges claim that they develop 'leaders.' All too often, that means turning out graduates who cannot feel fulfilled unless they are telling other people what to do. There are already too many people like that, and they are a menace to everyone else's freedom."

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010), p. 335*

title: Many issues are misconstrued, not because
grift: Many issues are misconstrued, not because
body:
## "Many issues are misconstrued, not because they are too complex for most people to understand, but because a mundane explanation is far less emotionally satisfying than an explanation which produces villains to hate and heroes to exalt."

Thomas Sowell, *Intellectuals and Society (2009), § The Dramatic*

title: Many on the political left
grift: Many on the political left
body:
## "Many on the political left are so entranced by the beauty of their vision that they cannot see the ugly reality they are creating in the real world."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: Morally and intellectually superior
grift: Morally and intellectually superior
body:
## "The real motives of liberals have nothing to do with the welfare of other people. Instead, they have two related goals — to establish themselves as morally and intellectually superior to the rather distasteful population of common people, and to gather as much power as possible to tell those distasteful common people how they must live their lives."

Thomas Sowell

title: More frightening than any particular beliefs
grift: More frightening than any particular beliefs
body:
## "More frightening than any particular beliefs or policies is an utter lack of any sense of a need to test those beliefs and policies against hard evidence. Mistakes can be corrected by those who pay attention to facts but dogmatism will not be corrected by those who are wedded to a vision."

Thomas Sowell, *Who Really Cares? (2006)*

title: More whites were brought as slaves
grift: More whites were brought as slaves
body:
## "More whites were brought as slaves to North Africa than blacks brought as slaves to the United States or to the 13 colonies from which it was formed. White slaves were still being bought and sold in the Ottoman Empire, decades after blacks were freed in the United States."

Thomas Sowell, *The Real History of Slavery (2004)*

title: Most people on the right have
grift: Most people on the right have
body:
## "Most people on the right have no problem understanding people on the left because many, if not most, were on the left themselves when they were younger. But many, if not most, people on the left find it inexplicable how any decent and intelligent person could be on the right."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: Most people on the right have
grift: Most people on the right have
body:
## "Most people on the right have no problem understanding people on the left because many, if not most, were on the left themselves when they were younger. But many, if not most, people on the left find it inexplicable how any decent and intelligent person could be on the right."

Thomas Sowell, *Some random thoughts on the right, the left, Mad and more (2008)*

title: Much of the social history of
grift: Much of the social history of
body:
## "Much of the social history of the Western world over the past three decades has involved replacing what worked with what sounded good."

Thomas Sowell, *Is Reality Optional (1993), § SOCIAL DETERIORATION*

title: Much of what are called 'social problems'
grift: Much of what are called 'social problems'
body:
## "Much of what are called 'social problems' consists of the fact that intellectuals have theories that do not fit the real world. From this they conclude that it is the real world which is wrong and needs changing."

Thomas Sowell

title: Multiculturalism, like the caste system
grift: Multiculturalism, like the caste system
body:
## "Multiculturalism, like the caste system, tends to freeze people where the accident of birth has placed them."

Thomas Sowell

title: No government of the left has
grift: No government of the left has
body:
## "No government of the left has done as much for the poor as capitalism has. Even when it comes to the redistribution of income, the left talks the talk but the free market walks the walk. What do the poor most need? They need to stop being poor. And how can that be done, on a mass scale, except by an economy that creates vastly more wealth? Yet the political left has long had a remarkable lack of interest in how wealth is created. As far as they are concerned, wealth exists somehow and the only interesting question is how to redistribute it."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: No matter how disastrously some policy
grift: No matter how disastrously some policy
body:
## "No matter how disastrously some policy has turned out, anyone who criticizes it can expect to hear: 'But what would you replace it with?' When you put out a fire, what do you replace it with?"

Thomas Sowell, *Is Reality Optional (1993)*

title: No matter what you say, there
grift: No matter what you say, there
body:
## "No matter what you say, there are people who will hear only what they want to hear. Many of the heated political controversies of our times can be traced to that simple fact."

Thomas Sowell, *Barbarians Inside the Gates and Other Controversial Essays (1999)*

title: No society ever thrived because it
grift: No society ever thrived because it
body:
## "No society ever thrived because it had a large and growing class of parasites living off those who produce. On the contrary, the growth of a large parasitic class marked the decline and fall of the Roman Empire and the collapse of Spain from the heights of its golden age."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § POWER TO THE PARASITES*

title: Nobody is equal to anybody
grift: Nobody is equal to anybody
body:
## "Nobody is equal to anybody. Even the same man is not equal to himself on different days."

Thomas Sowell

title: Of all ignorance, the ignorance of
grift: Of all ignorance, the ignorance of
body:
## "Of all ignorance, the ignorance of the educated is the most dangerous.

Not only are educated people likely to have more influence, they are the last people to suspect that they don't know what they are talking about."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011)*

title: One of many disservices done to young people
grift: One of many disservices done to young people
body:
## "One of many disservices done to young people is giving them the puffed up notion that they are in a position to pass sweeping judgments on a world that they have barely begun to experience."

Thomas Sowell, *Cheering Immaturity (2010)*

title: One of the bittersweet things about
grift: One of the bittersweet things about
body:
## "One of the bittersweet things about growing old is realizing how mistaken you were when you were young. As a young political leftist, I saw the left as the voice of the common man. Nothing could be further from the truth."

Thomas Sowell, *Is Reality Optional (1993)*

title: One of the most important reasons
grift: One of the most important reasons
body:
## "One of the most important reasons for studying history is that virtually every stupid idea that is in vogue today has been tried before and proved disastrous before, time and again."

Thomas Sowell, *Jewish World Review Column by Thomas Sowell (2001)*

title: One of the most remarkable feats
grift: One of the most remarkable feats
body:
## "One of the most remarkable feats of those with the vision of the anointed has been the maintenance of their reputations in the face of repeated predictions that proved to be wrong by miles."

Thomas Sowell, *The Vision of the Anointed: Self-Congratulation as a Basis for Social Policy (1995), Ch. 4*

title: One of the reasons it has
grift: One of the reasons it has
body:
## "One of the reasons it has taken so long for some people to finally see through Barack Obama is that people do not like to admit, even to themselves, that they have been played for fools by a slick-talking politician."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: One of the sad signs of
grift: One of the sad signs of
body:
## "One of the sad signs of our times is that we have demonized those who produce, subsidized those who refuse to produce, and canonized those who complain."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § Random Thoughts*

title: Our children and grandchildren may yet
grift: Our children and grandchildren may yet
body:
## "Our children and grandchildren may yet curse the day we began hyping race and ethnicity. There are countries where that has led to slaughters in the streets but you cannot name a country where it has led to greater harmony."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: Our tax system penalizes those who
grift: Our tax system penalizes those who
body:
## "Our tax system penalizes those who are producing wealth in order to subsidize those who are only consuming it."

Thomas Sowell, *Ever Wonder Why? And Other Controversial Essays (2006)*

title: Ours may become the first civilization destroyed
grift: Ours may become the first civilization destroyed
body:
## "Ours may become the first civilization destroyed, not by the power of our enemies, but by the ignorance of our teachers and the dangerous nonsense they are teaching our children."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: People in minimum wage jobs do
grift: People in minimum wage jobs do
body:
## "People in minimum wage jobs do not stay at the minimum wage permanently. Their pay increases as they accumulate experience and develop skills. It increases an average of 30 percent in just their first year of employment, according to the Cato Institute study. Other studies show that low-income people become average-income people in a few years and high-income people later in life.

All of this depends on their having a job in the first place, however. But the living wage kills jobs."

Thomas Sowell, *Ever Wonder Why? And Other Controversial Essays (2006)*

title: People who are very aware
grift: People who are very aware
body:
## "People who are very aware that they have more knowledge than the average person are often very unaware that they do not have one-tenth of the knowledge of all of the average persons put together. In this situation, for the intelligentsia to impose their notions on ordinary people is essentially to impose ignorance on knowledge."

Thomas Sowell

title: People who pride themselves
grift: People who pride themselves
body:
## "People who pride themselves on their 'complexity' and deride others for being 'simplistic' should realize that the truth is often not very complicated. What gets complex is evading the truth."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011)*

title: People who speak disdainfully of 'menial'
grift: People who speak disdainfully of 'menial'
body:
## "People who speak disdainfully of 'menial' jobs probably have no idea how many millionaires and even billionaires began working in such jobs. The political left is so wedded to their assumption that people stay in their 'class' that it would never occur to them to check out the hard facts."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: People will forgive you for being
grift: People will forgive you for being
body:
## "People will forgive you for being wrong, but they will never forgive you for being right, especially when events prove you right while proving them wrong."

Thomas Sowell, *Is Reality Optional (1993), § Random Thoughts*

title: People with no experience in business
grift: People with no experience in business
body:
## "People with no experience in business, no knowledge of history, and utterly ignorant of economics do not hesitate to leap from high prices to greedy profit-makers. Many of these ignorant people are on nationwide television and some are in Congress."

Thomas Sowell, *Posturing over the price of gas (2006)*

title: Perhaps the most dangerous favor done
grift: Perhaps the most dangerous favor done
body:
## "Perhaps the most dangerous ‘favor’ done to blacks has been the making of excuses for all their problems.

All human beings are so imperfect, no matter what color wrapping they come in, that to exempt any group from the standards of performance and behavior expected of others is not a blessing but a curse.

Many white liberals have adopted blacks as mascots, in order to ‘make a statement’ against American society.

But mascots are only symbols — and their well-being is seldom a top priority."

Thomas Sowell

title: Propagandists in the classroom are a
grift: Propagandists in the classroom are a
body:
## "Propagandists in the classroom are a luxury that the poor can afford least of all. While a mastery of mathematics and English can be a ticket out of poverty, a highly cultivated sense of grievance and resentment is not."

Thomas Sowell, *Charter Schools and Their Enemies (2020)*

title: Racism is not dead
grift: Racism is not dead
body:
## "Racism is not dead, but it is on life support — kept alive by politicians, race hustlers and people who get a sense of superiority by denouncing others as 'racists'."

Thomas Sowell

title: Ronald Reagan had a vision of
grift: Ronald Reagan had a vision of
body:
## "Ronald Reagan had a vision of America. Barack Obama has a vision of Barack Obama."

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010), p. 334*

title: Since wealth is the only thing
grift: Since wealth is the only thing
body:
## "Since wealth is the only thing that can cure poverty, you might think that the left would be as obsessed with the creation of wealth as they are with the redistribution of wealth. But you would be wrong."

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010)*
**_That's because they don't care about eradicating poverty they want power._**

title: Slavery existed all over the world
grift: Slavery existed all over the world
body:
## "Slavery existed all over the world for thousands of years among all sorts of people, as far back as the history of the human species goes.

It's one of many evils that the Left tries to localize, when in fact it is a universal evil."

Thomas Sowell, _Video Interview_

title: Slavery has existed all over the
grift: Slavery has existed all over the
body:
## "Slavery has existed all over the planet for thousands of years, with black, white, yellow and other races being both slaves and enslavers. Does that mean that everybody ought to apologize to everybody else for what their ancestors did?"

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010), p. 238*

title: Slavery has existed all over the
grift: Slavery has existed all over the
body:
## "Slavery has existed all over the planet for thousands of years, with black, white, yellow and other races being both slaves and enslavers. Does that mean that everybody ought to apologize to everybody else for what their ancestors did? Or are the only people who are supposed to feel guilty the ones who have money that others want to talk them out of?"

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010)*

title: Slavery was not confined by race.
grift: Slavery was not confined by race.
body:
## "Slavery was not confined by race. It was not defined or created by race. It existed for thousands of years.

A history professor once had a student come up to him and ask, ‘When did slavery begin?’ The real question is: when did freedom begin? Slavery existed as long as we have records. From archaeological findings, we know that people were enslaving other people before they could read or write. It has always existed and has existed all over the world.

The number of White people enslaved by pirates in North Africa was greater than the number of Africans brought to the United States.

And yet, that’s not even mentioned."

Thomas Sowell, *C-SPAN Interview*

title: So many idealistic political movements for
grift: So many idealistic political movements for
body:
## "So many idealistic political movements for a better world have ended in mass-murdering dictatorships. 

Giving leaders enough power to create ‘social justice’ is giving them enough power to destroy all justice, all freedom, and all human dignity."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: Socialism in general has a record of failure
grift: Socialism in general has a record of failure
body:
## "Socialism in general has a record of failure so blatant that only an intellectual could ignore or evade"

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011)*

title: Some Americans will never appreciate America
grift: Some Americans will never appreciate America
body:
## "Some Americans will never appreciate America, until after they have helped destroy it, and have then begun to suffer the consequences."

Thomas Sowell

title: Some of the biggest cases of
grift: Some of the biggest cases of
body:
## "Some of the biggest cases of mistaken identity are among intellectuals who have trouble remembering that they are not god."

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010)*

title: Some people are puzzled by the
grift: Some people are puzzled by the
body:
## "Some people are puzzled by the fact that so much that is said and done by politicians seems remote from reality. But reality is not what gets politicians elected. Appearances, rhetoric and emotions are what get them elected. Reality is what the voters and taxpayers are left to deal with, as a result of electing them."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: Some things are believed because they
grift: Some things are believed because they
body:
## "Some things are believed because they are demonstrably true. But many other things are believed simply because they have been asserted repeatedly—and repetition has been accepted as a substitute for evidence."

Thomas Sowell

title: Some things must be done on
grift: Some things must be done on
body:
## "Some things must be done on faith, but the most dangerous kind of faith is that which masquerades as ‘science.’"

Thomas Sowell, *Is Reality Optional (1993)*

title: Subjecting whole populations to the fancies
grift: Subjecting whole populations to the fancies
body:
## "Subjecting whole populations to the fancies of intellectuals and politicians has repeatedly proved deadly. That lesson has been written in blood across the history of the twentieth century."

Thomas Sowell, *The Quest for Cosmic Justice (1999)*

title: Teachers who think that they have
grift: Teachers who think that they have
body:
## "Teachers who think that they have a right to use other people's children as guinea pigs for social experiments should be fired."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: That people on the political left
grift: That people on the political left
body:
## "That people on the political left have a certain set of opinions… is not surprising. What is surprising, however, is how often the opinions of those on the left are accompanied by hostility and even hatred."
