Thomas Sowell, *The Anger Of The Left (2007)*

title: The arguments against charter schools are
grift: The arguments against charter schools are
body:
## "The arguments against charter schools are so phony that anyone with a decent education should be able to see right through them. Unfortunately, the very failure of many traditional public schools to provide a decent education enables their defenders to get away with arguments that could not survive any serious analysis."

Thomas Sowell, *Moral Bankruptcy (2018)*

title: The assumption that spending more of
grift: The assumption that spending more of
body:
## "The assumption that spending more of the taxpayers' money will make things better has survived all kinds of evidence that it has made things worse."

Thomas Sowell, *Is Reality Optional (1993)*

title: The average American today has a
grift: The average American today has a
body:
## "The average American today has a standard of living that includes things that only the upper crust could have afforded in times past—and some things that even the rich didn’t have in past generations, like personal computers.

But are the people who made that possible even mentioned, much less publicized and praised?

There is not an inventor, scientist, medical researcher, or industrialist who is as well known as loudmouths like Rosie O’Donnell or Jesse Jackson.

Any bimbo who exposes her body can get more attention than someone who finds ways to reduce the cost of housing for millions of people. In California, the bimbo can get favorable attention while the developer is condemned."

Thomas Sowell

title: The barbarians are not at the
grift: The barbarians are not at the
body:
## "The barbarians are not at the gates. They are INSIDE THE GATES and have academic tenure, judicial appointments, government grants, and control of the movies, television, and other media."

Thomas Sowell, *Barbarians Inside the Gates and Other Controversial Essays (1999)*

title: The beauty of doing nothing
grift: The beauty of doing nothing
body:
## "The beauty of doing nothing is that you can do it perfectly. Only when you do something is it almost impossible to do it without mistakes. Therefore people who are contributing nothing to society, except their constant criticisms, can feel both intellectually and morally superior."

Thomas Sowell

title: The biggest and most deadly tax
grift: The biggest and most deadly tax
body:
## "The biggest and most deadly ‘tax’ rate on the poor comes from a loss of various welfare state benefits – food stamps, housing subsidies and the like – if their income goes up."

Thomas Sowell

title: The biggest difference between the left
grift: The biggest difference between the left
body:
## "The biggest difference between the left and right today, when it comes to racial issues, is that liberals tend to take the side of those blacks who are doing the wrong things — hoodlums the left depicts as martyrs, while the right defends those blacks more likely to be the victims of those hoodlums."

Thomas Sowell

title: The biggest hypocrites on gun control
grift: The biggest hypocrites on gun control
body:
## "The biggest hypocrites on gun control are those who live in upscale developments with armed security guards – and who want to keep other people from having guns to defend themselves. But what about lower-income people living in high-crime, inner city neighborhoods? Should such people be kept unarmed and helpless, so that limousine liberals can ’make a statement’ by adding to the thousands of gun laws already on the books?"

Thomas Sowell

title: The biggest myth about labor unions
grift: The biggest myth about labor unions
body:
## "The biggest myth about labor unions is that unions are for the workers. Unions are for unions, just as corporations are for corporations and politicians are for politicians."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011)*

title: The black family which survived slavery
grift: The black family which survived slavery
body:
## "The black family survived centuries of slavery and generations of segregation, but it has disintegrated in the wake of the Liberals' expansion of the welfare state."

Thomas Sowell

title: The combination of Lenins genius for
grift: The combination of Lenins genius for
body:
## "The combination of Lenin’s genius for propaganda and an audience receptive to his thesis allowed his theory of imperialism to be widely accepted among intellectuals, activists, and people in the Third World. Exploitation is a virtually perfect explanation of income differences. It validates whatever envy or resentment may be felt by people with lower incomes toward people with higher incomes. It removes whatever stigma can be felt from implications of lower ability or lower performance on the part of those with lower incomes. It locates the need for change in other people, rather than imposing the burden of change on those who wish to rise. Moreover, it replaces any such burdensome task with a morally uplifting sense of entitlement. Whatever the empirical and logical problems with the theory of exploitation, political movements are seldom based on empirical evidence and logic."

Thomas Sowell, *Economic Facts and Fallacies (2008)*

title: The concept of microaggression is just
grift: The concept of microaggression is just
body:
## "The concept of ‘microaggression’ is just one of many tactics used to stifle differences of opinion by declaring some opinions to be ‘hate speech,’ instead of debating those differences in a marketplace of ideas. To accuse people of aggression for not marching in lockstep with political correctness is to set the stage for justifying real aggression against them."

Thomas Sowell, *Micro-totalitarianism (2015)*

title: The dirty little secret of liberal
grift: The dirty little secret of liberal
body:
## "The dirty little secret of liberal politics is that it is not about the poor or "social justice" but is about the political careers and moral exaltation of liberals themselves.

The actual consequences of liberal policies on the poor or others seldom receives anything like the amount of attention given to promoting these policies and demonizing the critics of these politics."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2012)*

title: The dogma that no culture is
grift: The dogma that no culture is
body:
## "The dogma that no culture is superior to any other is contradicted by facts at every turn. Virtually every culture is superior to some other cultures at something. How many cultures can brew beer like the Germans, create wine like the French or make Sake like the Japanese?"

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: The endlessly repeated argument that most
grift: The endlessly repeated argument that most
body:
## "The endlessly repeated argument that most Americans are the descendants of immigrants ignores the fact that most Americans are NOT the descendants of ILLEGAL immigrants."

Thomas Sowell, *Immigration and Illegal Immigration (2006)*

title: The engineer is judged by the
grift: The engineer is judged by the
body:
## "The engineer is judged by the end product: if he builds a building that collapses, he's ruined. Conversely, if an intellectual who is brilliant has an idea for rearranging society and it ends in disaster, he pays no price at all."

Thomas Sowell, *Thomas Sowell on Intellectuals and Society (2009)*

title: The fact that crime and poverty
grift: The fact that crime and poverty
body:
## "The fact that crime and poverty are correlated is automatically taken to mean that poverty causes crime, rather than that similar attitudes or behavior patterns may contribute to both poverty and crime."

Thomas Sowell

title: The fact that so many successful
grift: The fact that so many successful
body:
## "The fact that so many successful politicians are such shameless liars is not only a reflection on them, it is also a reflection on us. When the people want the impossible, only liars can satisfy them, and only in the short run."

Thomas Sowell, *Big Lies in Politics (2012)*

title: The fatal attraction of government is
grift: The fatal attraction of government is
body:
## "The fatal attraction of government is that it allows busybodies to impose decisions on others without paying any price themselves. That enables them to act as if there were no price, even when there are ruinous prices — paid by others."

Thomas Sowell, _Ever Wonder Why? And Other Controversial Essays (2006), p. 225 (Hoover Institution Press)_

title: The first lesson of economics is scarcity
grift: The first lesson of economics is scarcity
body:
## "The first lesson of economics is scarcity: there is never enough of anything to fully satisfy all those who want it. The first lesson of politics is to disregard the first lesson of economics."

Thomas Sowell, *Is Reality Optional (1993)*

title: The Fourteenth Amendment provides for equal
grift: The Fourteenth Amendment provides for equal
body:
## "The Fourteenth Amendment provides for equal rights and equal protection of the laws. If you want more than that, then you are no longer talking about rights, but about special privileges."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002), § MURDER IS MURDER*

title: The fundamental difference between decision-makers in
grift: The fundamental difference between decision-makers in
body:
## "The fundamental difference between decision-makers in the market and decision-makers in government is that the former are subject to continuous and consequential feedback which can force them to adjust to what others prefer and are willing to pay for, while those who make decisions in the political arena face no such inescapable feedback to force them to adjust to the reality of other people’s desires and preferences."

Thomas Sowell, *Intellectuals and Society (2009)*

title: The fundamental problem of the political
grift: The fundamental problem of the political
body:
## "The fundamental problem of the political Left seems to be that the real world does not fit their preconceptions.

Therefore, they see the real world as what is wrong and what needs to be changed, since apparently their preconceptions cannot be wrong."

Thomas Sowell, *The Mindset of the Left: Part III (2013)*

title: The great danger to liberty
grift: The great danger to liberty
body:
## "The great danger to liberty is that the state will come to regard all liberty as a threat to itself."

Thomas Sowell

title: The great ideological divide is between
grift: The great ideological divide is between
body:
## "The great ideological divide is between those who believe that theories should be adjusted to reality and those who believe that reality must be adjusted to fit their theories. Many of the horrors of the 20th century were created by the latter. And such people are still with us, in many movements."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: The human race began in poverty
grift: The human race began in poverty
body:
## "The human race began in poverty, so there is no mysterious explanation as to why some people are poor. The question is, why have some people gotten prosperous, and in particular why have some gotten prosperous to a greater degree than others."

Thomas Sowell, *Thomas Sowell on the second edition of Intellectuals and Society*

title: The idea that taxpayers owe it
grift: The idea that taxpayers owe it
body:
## "The idea that taxpayers owe it to you to pay for what you want suggests that much of today’s education fails to instill reality, and instead panders to a self-centered sense of entitlement to what other people have earned."

Thomas Sowell, *Political elites think they know more than they do (2010)*

title: The ideal of freedom behind the
grift: The ideal of freedom behind the
body:
## "The ideal of freedom behind the American Revolution had its effect in freeing thousands of people from slavery in the newly formed United States, something that was happening nowhere else in the world at that time. To call slavery “America’s original sin” is to turn reality upside down."

Thomas Sowell, *Wealth, Poverty and Politics (2015), Ch. 15*

title: The idealism of the Left is
grift: The idealism of the Left is
body:
## "The idealism of the Left is a very selfish one. In their war against 'the rich' and Big Business, they don't care how much collateral damage there is to workers who end up unemployed."

Thomas Sowell, *Taxes and Politics (2011)*

title: The ideas of the political left
grift: The ideas of the political left
body:
## "The ideas of the political left are not bad ideas. They just don't apply to human beings. That is why the left has always had to try to change human nature, whether through 're-education' camps 'brainwashing' in China, creating a "Soviet Man" in the U.S.S.R., or 'values clarification' programs in American schools."

Thomas Sowell, *Barbarians Inside the Gates and Other Controversial Essays (1999)*

title: The illusion that you can take
grift: The illusion that you can take
body:
## "The illusion that you can take in large numbers of people from a fundamentally different culture, without jeopardizing your own culture — and everything that depends on it — should have been dispelled by many counterproductive social consequences in Europe, even aside from the fatal dangers of terrorists."

Thomas Sowell

title: The last person to trust with
grift: The last person to trust with
body:
## "The last person to trust with power is someone who is dying to have it. The best person to wield power is someone who is reluctant to do so, but who will do it for a while as a civic duty. That is why term limits should make it impossible to have a whole career in politics."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § REFLECTIONS ON TERM LIMITS*

title: The left takes its vision seriously
grift: The left takes its vision seriously
body:
## "The left takes its vision seriously — more seriously than it takes the rights of other people. They want to be our shepherds, but that requires us to be sheep."

Thomas Sowell

title: The more I study the history of
grift: The more I study the history of
body:
## "The more I study the history of intellectuals, the more they seem like a wrecking crew, dismantling civilization bit by bit — replacing what works with what sounds good."

Thomas Sowell

title: The most basic question is
grift: The most basic question is
body:
## "The most basic question is not what is best, but who shall decide what is best."

Thomas Sowell, *Knowledge and Decisions (1980), p. 79*

title: The most fundamental fact about the
grift: The most fundamental fact about the
body:
## "The most fundamental fact about the ideas of the political left is that they do not work. Therefore we should not be surprised to find the left concentrated in institutions where ideas do not have to work in order to survive."

Thomas Sowell, *The Survival of the Left (1997)*

title: The next time some academics tell you
grift: The next time some academics tell you
body:
## "The next time some academics tell you how important diversity is, ask how many Republicans there are in their sociology department."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002), p. 318*

title: The number of Whites who were
grift: The number of Whites who were
body:
## "The number of Whites who were enslaved in North Africa by the Barbary pirates exceeded the number of Africans enslaved in the United States and in the American colonies before that put together. But nobody is going to North Africa to ask for reparations."

Thomas Sowell, *Maverick: Jason Riley On The Life And Times Of Thomas Sowell (2020)*

title: The only way anyone can have
grift: The only way anyone can have
body:
## "The only way anyone can have a right to something that has to be produced is to force someone else to produce it for him. The more things are provided as rights, the less the recipients have to work and the more others have to carry their load."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § "AFFORDABILITY"*

title: The politically correct party line on
grift: The politically correct party line on
body:
## "The politically correct party line on minimum wages is that people cannot afford to raise their families on low pay, so the government has to force employers to provide "a living wage" for families. But the vast majority of people making minimum wages are youngsters just beginning their careers."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: The problem isn't that Johnny can't
grift: The problem isn't that Johnny can't
body:
## "The problem isn't that Johnny can't read. The problem isn't even that Johnny can't think. The problem is that Johnny doesn't know what thinking is; he confuses it with feeling."

Thomas Sowell, *Inside American Education (1993), Ch. 1*

title: The purpose of politics
grift: The purpose of politics
body:
## "The purpose of politics is not to solve problems but to find problems to justify the expansion of government power and an increase in taxes."

Thomas Sowell

title: The real goal should be reduced
grift: The real goal should be reduced
body:
## "The real goal should be reduced government spending, rather than balanced budgets achieved by ever rising tax rates to cover ever rising spending."

Thomas Sowell, *Interview with John Hawkins (2012)*

title: The reason so many people misunderstand
grift: The reason so many people misunderstand
body:
## "The reason so many people misunderstand so many issues is not that these issues are so complex, but that people do not want a factual or analytical explanation that leaves them emotionally unsatisfied. They want villains to hate and heroes to cheer—and they don't want explanations that fail to give them that."

Thomas Sowell

title: The road to hell is paved
grift: The road to hell is paved
body:
## "The road to hell is paved with Ivy League degrees."

Thomas Sowell, *Uncommon Knowledge - Thomas Sowell on the second edition of Intellectuals and Society (2012)*

title: The soaring crime rates that followed
grift: The soaring crime rates that followed
body:
## "The soaring crime rates that followed the liberal nostrums of the 1960s have produced no change of mind. Liberals are often wrong but never in doubt. They are still blaming social conditions, “society,” America.

It will never occur to them to blame themselves."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § PASSING IN REVIEW*

title: The totalitarian mindset behind the liberal
grift: The totalitarian mindset behind the liberal
body:
## "The totalitarian mindset behind the liberal vision shows through in innumerable ways. There are no institutions in America where free speech is more severely restricted than in our politically correct colleges and universities, dominated by liberals."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § "Forced to Volunteer"*

title: The utterly un-self-critical attitude of many
grift: The utterly un-self-critical attitude of many
body:
## "The utterly un-self-critical attitude of many intellectuals has survived many demonstrably vast, and even grotesque, contrasts between their notions and the realities of the world."

Thomas Sowell, *Intellectuals and Society (2009)*

title: The very same people who say
grift: The very same people who say
body:
## "The very same people who say that government has no right to interfere with sexual activity between consenting adults believe that the government has every right to interfere with economic activity between consenting adults."

Thomas Sowell

title: The welfare state is not really about the welfare of the masses
grift: The welfare state is not really about the welfare of the masses
body:
## "The welfare state is not really about the welfare of the masses. It is about the egos of the elites."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § Human Livestock*

title: The welfare state is the greatest
grift: The welfare state is the greatest
body:
## "The welfare state is the greatest confidence racket of all time. The government takes your money in taxes and then turns around and spends some of it to give you things. For this, you feel dependent on them, when in fact they are dependent on you."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002), p. 306*

title: The welfare state is the oldest con game
grift: The welfare state is the oldest con game
body:
## "The welfare state is the oldest con game in the world. First you take people's money away quietly and then you give some of it back to them flamboyantly."

Thomas Sowell

title: The world of the anointed is
grift: The world of the anointed is
body:
## "The world of the anointed is a very tidy place—or, put differently, every deviation of the real world from the tidiness of their vision is considered to be someone’s fault. If employment or college admissions statistics do not match the preconceptions of the anointed, then that indicates discrimination."

Thomas Sowell

title: There are few things more dishonorable
grift: There are few things more dishonorable
body:
## "There are few things more dishonorable than misleading the young."

Thomas Sowell, _Controversial Essays (2002)_

title: There are no solutions, only trade-offs
grift: There are no solutions, only trade-offs
body:
## "There are no solutions, only trade-offs."

Thomas Sowell

title: There are no solutions
grift: There are no solutions
body:
## "There are no solutions. There are only trade-offs."

Thomas Sowell

title: There are people who can neither
grift: There are people who can neither
body:
## "There are people who can neither find happiness in their own lives nor permit those around them to be happy. The best you can do is get such people out of your life."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § Random Thoughts*

title: There are people who will not
grift: There are people who will not
body:
## "There are people who will not stop until they get stopped — and much of the media, the political classes and the cultural elites of the West cannot bring themselves to even criticize, much less stop, the dangers or degeneracy among groups viewed sympathetically as underdogs."

Thomas Sowell

title: There has now been created a world
grift: There has now been created a world
body:
## "There has now been created a world in which the success of others is a grievance, rather than an example."

Thomas Sowell

title: There have always been ignorant people
grift: There have always been ignorant people
body:
## "There have always been ignorant people but they haven't always had college degrees to make them unaware of their ignorance some people imagine that they are well informed because they have memorized a whole galaxy of trendy dogmas and fashionable attitudes."

Thomas Sowell

title: There is no greater indictment of
grift: There is no greater indictment of
body:
## "There is no greater indictment of judges than the fact that honest people are afraid to go into court, while criminals swagger out its revolving doors."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011)*

title: There is nothing that politicians like
grift: There is nothing that politicians like
body:
## "There is nothing that politicians like better than handing out benefits to be paid for by someone else."

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010)*

title: There is really nothing very mysterious
grift: There is really nothing very mysterious
body:
## "There is really nothing very mysterious about why our public schools are failures. When you select the poorest quality college students to be public school teachers, give them iron-clad tenure, a captive audience, and pay them according to seniority rather than performance, why should the results be surprising?"

Thomas Sowell, *Is Reality Optional (1993)*

title: There was a time when we
grift: There was a time when we
body:
## "There was a time when we honored those who created the prosperity and the freedom that we enjoy. Today we honor the complainers and sue the creators. Perhaps that is inevitable in an era when we no longer count our blessings, but instead count all our unfulfilled wishes."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002)*

title: Those who are constantly looking for
grift: Those who are constantly looking for
body:
## "Those who are constantly looking for the 'root causes' of poverty, of crime, and of other national and international problems, act as if prosperity and law-abiding behavior are so natural that it is their absence which has to be explained. But a casual glance around the world today, or back through history, would dispel any notion that good things just happen naturally, much less inevitably."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § Every Wonder Why?*

title: Those who are primarily concerned about
grift: Those who are primarily concerned about
body:
## "Those who are primarily concerned about the well-being of the poor are likely to discover over time that much of the agenda of the left does not really do much good for the poor, and some of that agenda—environmental extremism, for example—actually makes the poor worse off."

Thomas Sowell

title: Those who cry out that the government should 'do something'
grift: Those who cry out that the government should 'do something'
body:
## "Those who cry out that the government should 'do something' never even ask what the government is qualified to do."

Thomas Sowell

title: Those who want to see quality
grift: Those who want to see quality
body:
## "Those who want to see quality education remain available to youngsters in low-income minority neighborhoods must raise the question, again and again, when various policies and practices are proposed: 'How is this going to affect the education of children?' Surprisingly large proportion of policies and practices cannot answer that question."

Thomas Sowell, *Charter Schools and Their Enemies (2020)*

title: Thousands of free blacks owned slaves
grift: Thousands of free blacks owned slaves
body:
## "Thousands of free blacks owned slaves in the antebellum South. And, years after the Emancipation Proclamation in the United States, whites as well as blacks were still being bought and sold as slaves in North Africa and the Middle East."

Thomas Sowell, *Barbarians Inside the Gates and Other Controversial Essays (1999), § THE SLAVERY CARD*

title: To understand intellectuals' role in society
grift: To understand intellectuals' role in society
body:
## "To understand intellectuals' role in society, we must look beyond their rhetoric to the reality of their revealed preferences."

Thomas Sowell, *Intellectuals and Society (2009)*

title: Too much of what is called education
grift: Too much of what is called education
body:
## "Too much of what is called 'education' is little more than an expensive isolation from reality"

Thomas Sowell

title: Unfortunately, the real minimum wage is
grift: Unfortunately, the real minimum wage is
body:
## "Unfortunately, the real minimum wage is always zero, regardless of the laws, and that is the wage that many workers receive in the wake of the creation or escalation of a government-mandated minimum wage, because they lose their jobs or fail to find jobs when they enter the labor force. Making it illegal to pay less than a given amount does not make a worker’s productivity worth that amount—and, if it is not, that worker is unlikely to be employed."

Thomas Sowell

title: Virtually every stupid idea in vogue
grift: Virtually every stupid idea in vogue
body:
## "Virtually every stupid idea in vogue today was thought of by somebody in the past — and has led to disaster, again and again. That is why it is dangerous to neglect the study of history, so that we have to keep on learning what is wrong with clever ideas the hard way. Do we need to keep repeating the same mistakes forever?"

Thomas Sowell

title: Virtually everybody is worse off than
grift: Virtually everybody is worse off than
body:
## "Virtually everybody is worse off than somebody else, if only in one dimension, so there are nearly unlimited opportunities to pander to people's sense of injustice, victimhood, and entitlement."

Thomas Sowell, *Hard Times for Envy (2003)*

title: War makes me respect soldiers and
grift: War makes me respect soldiers and
body:
## "War makes me respect soldiers and despise politicians."

Thomas Sowell, *Controversial Essays (2002), p. 321*

title: We all enter the world knowing
grift: We all enter the world knowing
body:
## "We all enter the world knowing nothing but, by the time we are teenagers, we know it all. Sometimes it is decades later before we know enough to realize how little we know."

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010), p. 334*

title: We are a nation of immigrants
grift: We are a nation of immigrants
body:
## ""We are a nation of immigrants," we are constantly reminded. We are also a nation of people with ten fingers and ten toes. Does that mean that anyone who has ten fingers and ten toes should be welcomed and given American citizenship?"

Thomas Sowell

title: We are among the biggest fools in history
grift: We are among the biggest fools in history
body:
## "We are among the biggest fools in history if we keep on paying people to make us hate each other. Whether it is calledby pretty names like 'multiculturalism','diverfsity', or 'gender awareness,' that is what it all boils down"

Thomas Sowell, *Barbarians Inside the Gates and Other Controversial Essays (1999)*

title: We do not have a choice
grift: We do not have a choice
body:
## "We do not have a choice whether or not to discuss history. History has always been invoked in contemporary controversies. The only choice is between discussing what actually happened in the past and discussing notions projected into the past for present purposes. History is the memory of the human race. For an individual to wake up some morning with no memory would be devastating."

Thomas Sowell, *Black Rednecks and White Liberals (2005), p. 276*

title: Welfare states on both sides
grift: Welfare states on both sides
body:
## "Welfare states on both sides of the Atlantic have discovered that largesse to losers does not reduce their hostility to society, but only increases it. Far from producing gratitude, generosity is seen as an admission of guilt, and the reparations as inadequate compensations for injustices - leading to worsening behavior by the recipients."

Thomas Sowell

title: Western civilization has survived the invasions
grift: Western civilization has survived the invasions
body:
## "Western civilization has survived the invasions of Genghis Khan from the East, the Ottoman Empire from the South, and two world wars originating from within. But whether it will survive its own intellectuals is much more doubtful."

Thomas Sowell, *Is Reality Optional (1993)*

title: What do you call it when someone steals money
grift: What do you call it when someone steals money
body:
## "What do you call it when someone steals money secretly? Theft. What do you call it when someone takes money by force? Robbery. What do you call it when a politician takes someone else's money and gives it to someone likely to vote for him? Social Justice."

Thomas Sowell

title: What exactly is your fair share
grift: What exactly is your fair share
body:
## "What exactly is your fair share of what someone else has worked for?"

Thomas Sowell, *Dismantling America: And Other Controversial Essays (2010)*

title: What 'multiculturalism' boils down to
grift: What 'multiculturalism' boils down to
body:
## "What 'multiculturalism' boils down to is that you can praise any culture in the world except Western culture-and you cannot blame any culture in the world except Western culture."

Thomas Sowell

title: What was peculiar about the West
grift: What was peculiar about the West
body:
## "What was peculiar about the West was not that it participated in the worldwide evil of slavery, but that it later abolished that evil, not only in Western societies but also in other societies subject to Western control or influence. 

This was possible only because the anti-slavery movement coincided with an era in which Western power and hegemony were at their zenith, so that it was essentially European imperialism which ended slavery. This idea might seem shocking, not because it does not fit the facts, but because it does not fit the prevailing vision of our time."

Thomas Sowell, *Black Rednecks and White Liberals (2005), p. 134-135*

title: When I was growing up
grift: When I was growing up
body:
## "When I was growing up, we were taught the stories of people whose inventions and scientific discoveries had expanded the lives of millions of other people. Today, students are being taught to admire those who complain, denounce and demand."

Thomas Sowell

title: When it comes to lifting people
grift: When it comes to lifting people
body:
## "When it comes to lifting people out of poverty, redistribution of income has a much poorer track record than the creation of wealth. In some places, such as Zimbabwe today, attempts at a redistribution of wealth have turned out to be a redistribution of poverty.

## While the creation of wealth may be more effective for enabling millions of people to rise out of poverty, it provides no special role for the political left, no puffed-up importance, no moral superiority. Redistribution is clearly better for the left."

Thomas Sowell, _The Cure for Poverty? Wealth (2006)_

title: When only two percent of immigrants
grift: When only two percent of immigrants
body:
## "When only two percent of immigrants from Japan to the United States go on welfare, while 46 percent of the immigrants from Laos do, there is no single pattern that applies to all immigrants. Everything depends on which immigrants you are talking about and which periods of history."

Thomas Sowell, *Basic Economics (2000)*

title: When politicians say 'spread the wealth'
grift: When politicians say 'spread the wealth'
body:
## "When politicians say 'spread the wealth,' translate that as 'concentrate the power,' because that is the only way they can spread the wealth. And once they get the power concentrated, they can do anything else they want to, as people have discovered - often to their horror - in countries around the world."

Thomas Sowell

title: When the only external validation for
grift: When the only external validation for
body:
## "When the only external validation for an individual is what other individuals believe, everything depends on who those other individuals are. If they are simply people who are like-minded in general, then the consensus of the group about a new idea depends on what the group already believes in general—and says nothing about the empirical validity of that idea in the external world."

Thomas Sowell

title: When we have a system where
grift: When we have a system where
body:
## "When we have a system where children are fed in schools and other basic responsibilities are also lifted from the shoulders of their parents, why should we be surprised that the sense of parental responsibility seems to be eroding? We are not surprised when a couch potato doesn’t have the kind of muscles found on someone who exercises. Our society is increasingly turning out moral couch potatoes."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011), § BOOMERS AND BOOMERANGS*

title: When you consider what an enormous
grift: When you consider what an enormous
body:
## "When you consider what an enormous windfall gain it is to be born in America, it is painful to hear some people complain bitterly that someone else got a bigger windfall gain than they did."

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2001)*

title: When you see people starving in
grift: When you see people starving in
body:
## "When you see people starving in Venezuela and fleeing to neighboring countries and realize that this is a country that once had the world’s largest oil reserves, you realize that they’ve ruined a really good prospect with ideas that sounded good but didn’t turn out well."

Thomas Sowell, *Resurgent Thomas Sowell Weighs In on Socialism, Venezuela (2019)*

title: When you want to help someone
grift: When you want to help someone
body:
## "When you want to help someone, you'll tell them the truth. When you want to help yourself, you'll tell them what they want to hear."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011)*

title: Where nobody is responsible for what they did
grift: Where nobody is responsible for what they did
body:
## "We seem to be getting closer and closer to a situation where nobody is responsible for what they did but we are all responsible for what somebody else did."

Thomas Sowell, *Ever Wonder Why? And Other Controversial Essays (2006), p. 454*

title: Whether blatant or subtle, brainwashing has
grift: Whether blatant or subtle, brainwashing has
body:
## "Whether blatant or subtle, brainwashing has become a major, time-consuming activity in American education at all levels. Some zealots have not hesitated to use the traditional brain-washing technique of emotional trauma in the classroom to soften up children for their message. Gruesome and graphic movies on nuclear war, for example, have reduced some school children to tears—after which the teacher makes a pitch for whatever movement claims to reduce such dangers. Another technique is the ambush shock: A seventh-grade teacher in Manhattan, for example, innocently asked her students to discuss their future plans—after which she said: ‘Haven’t any of you realized that in this world with nuclear weapons no one in this class will be alive in the year 2000?’"

Thomas Sowell

title: Whether in politics or in the
grift: Whether in politics or in the
body:
## "Whether in politics or in the media, words are increasingly used, not to convey facts or even allegations of facts, but simply to arouse emotions. Undefined words are a big handicap in logic, but they are a big plus in politics, where the goal is not clarity but victory — and the votes of gullible people count just as much as the votes of people who have common sense."

Thomas Sowell

title: While President Barack Obama has, in
grift: While President Barack Obama has, in
body:
## "While President Barack Obama has, in one sense, tipped his hand by saying that he wants judges with "empathy" for certain groups, he has in a more fundamental sense concealed the real goal - getting judges who will ratify an ever-expanding scope of the power of the federal government and an ever-declining restraint by the Constitution of the United States. This is consistent with everything else that Obama has done in office and is consistent with his decades-long track record of alliances with people who reject the fundamentals of American society."

Thomas Sowell, *Empathy Versus Law: Part IV (2009)*

title: While slavery was common to all
grift: While slavery was common to all
body:
## "While slavery was common to all civilizations, as well as to peoples considered uncivilized, only one civilization developed a moral revulsion against it, very late in its history—Western civilization."

Thomas Sowell, *Western civilization.*

title: White liberals, instead of comparing what
grift: White liberals, instead of comparing what
body:
## "White liberals, instead of comparing what has happened to the black family since the liberal welfare state policies of the 1960s were put into practice, compare black families to white families and conclude that the higher rates of broken homes and unwed motherhood among blacks are due to “a legacy of slavery.” But why the large-scale disintegration of the black family should have begun a hundred years after slavery is left unexplained. 

Whatever the situation of the black family relative to the white family, in the past or the present, it is clear that broken homes were far more common among blacks at the end of the twentieth century than they were in the middle of that century or at the beginning of that century —even though blacks at the beginning of the twentieth century were just one generation out of slavery. The widespread and casual abandonment of their children, and of the women who bore them, by black fathers in the ghettos of the late twentieth century was in fact a painfully ironic contrast with what had happened in the immediate aftermath of slavery a hundred years earlier, when observers in the South reported desperate efforts of freed blacks to find family members who had been separated from them during the era of slavery."

Thomas Sowell

title: Whites walk on eggshells for fear
grift: Whites walk on eggshells for fear
body:
## "Whites walk on eggshells for fear of being called racists, while many blacks are preoccupied with protecting the image of black students, rather than protecting their future by telling the blunt truth. It is understandable that some people are concerned about image, about what in private life might be expressed as: “What will the neighbors think?” But, when your children are dying, you don’t worry about what the neighbors think."

Thomas Sowell, *Black Rednecks and White Liberals (2005), p. 245*

title: Why anyone would think that criminals
grift: Why anyone would think that criminals
body:
## "Why anyone would think that criminals who disobey other laws, including laws against murder, would obey gun control laws is a mystery."

Thomas Sowell, *The 'Gun Control' Farce: Part II (2015)*

title: With all the talk about people
grift: With all the talk about people
body:
## "With all the talk about people paying their "fair share" of income taxes, why do nearly half the people in this country pay no income taxes at all? Is that their "fair share"? Or is creating more recipients of government handouts, at no cost to themselves, simply a strategy to gain more votes?"

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (2012)*

title: Would you bet your paycheck on
grift: Would you bet your paycheck on
body:
## "Would you bet your paycheck on a weather forecast for tomorrow?

If not, then why should this country bet billions on 'global warming' predictions that have even less foundation?"

Thomas Sowell, *Random Thoughts (1999)*

title: Yes. Some years ago, the politicians
grift: Yes. Some years ago, the politicians
body:
## "Yes. Some years ago, the politicians in Maryland decided they were going to raise more revenue by increasing the taxes on people who had an income of a million dollars a year or more. But after they did this, the number of people with incomes of a million dollars or more in Maryland went down. Because rich people were not chess pieces, they moved out of Maryland. Instead of getting $100 million in new tax revenue like they predicted, Maryland got $200 million less in revenue."

Thomas Sowell, *Thomas Sowell on Social Justice Fallacies: Racial Fallacies (2024)*

title: You can only confiscate the wealth
grift: You can only confiscate the wealth
body:
## "You can only confiscate the wealth that exists at a given moment. You cannot confiscate future wealth — and that future wealth is less likely to be produced when people see that it is going to be confiscated. 

Farmers in the Soviet Union cut back on how much time and effort they invested in growing their crops, when they realized that the government was going to take a big part of the harvest. They slaughtered and ate young animals that they would normally keep tending and feeding until maturity."

Thomas Sowell

title: You can only confiscate the wealth
grift: You can only confiscate the wealth
body:
## "You can only confiscate the wealth that exists at a given moment. You cannot confiscate future wealth — and that future wealth is less likely to be produced when people see that it is going to be confiscated."

Thomas Sowell

title: You have to have a sense of humor
grift: You have to have a sense of humor
body:
## "You have to have a sense of humor if you follow politics. Otherwise, the sheer fraudulence of it all will get you down."

Thomas Sowell, *Jewish World Review Column (2001)*

title: You might think that the collapse
grift: You might think that the collapse
body:
## "You might think that the collapse of communism throughout Eastern Europe would be considered a decisive failure for Marxism, but academic Marxists in America are utterly undaunted. Their paychecks and their tenure are unaffected. Their theories continue to flourish in the classrooms and their journals continue to litter the library shelves."

Thomas Sowell, *The Thomas Sowell Reader (2011)*

title: It constantly amazes me
grift: It constantly amazes me
body:
## "It constantly amazes me that defenders of the free market are expected to offer certainty and perfection, while government has only to make promises and offer good intentions."

Lawrence Reed

title: Marxism has led to Fascism
grift: Marxism has led to Fascism
body:
## "Marxism has led to Fascism and National Socialism, because, in all essentials, it is Fascism and National Socialism."

Friedrich Hayek

title: Prior to capitalism
grift: Prior to capitalism
body:
## "Prior to capitalism, the way people amassed great wealth was by looting, plundering and enslaving their fellow man. Capitalism made it possible to become wealthy by serving your fellow man."

Walter E Williams

title: Real Socialism' has never been tried
grift: Real Socialism' has never been tried
body:
## "'Real Socialism' has never been tried in the same way that 'Real Capitalism' has never been tried. The difference is 'Almost Socialism' resulted in the impoverishment & death of hundreds of millions of people. While 'Almost Capitalism' has lifted billions from absolute poverty."

@Mark Antro

title: The act of reaching into one's own pockets
grift: The act of reaching into one's own pockets
body:
## "The act of reaching into one's own pockets to help a fellow man in need is praiseworthy and laudable. Reaching into someone else's pocket is despicable and worthy of condemnation."

Walter E Williams

title: The history of the 20th century is full
grift: The history of the 20th century is full
body:
## "The history of the 20th century is full of examples of countries that set out to redistribute wealth and ended up redistributing poverty."

Thomas Sowell

title: The Constitution is not a living organism
grift: The Constitution is not a living organism
body:
## "The Constitution is not a living organism. It's a legal document, and it says what it says and doesn't say what it doesn't say."

Antonin Scalia

title: The Constitution was made to guard the people
grift: The Constitution was made to guard the people
body:
## "The Constitution was made to guard the people against the dangers of good intentions."

Daniel Webster

title: To live under the American Constitution
grift: To live under the American Constitution
body:
## "To live under the American Constitution is the greatest political privilege that was ever accorded to the human race."

Calvin Coolidge

title: Can a people tax themselves into prosperity?
grift: Can a people tax themselves into prosperity?
body:
## "Can a people tax themselves into prosperity? Can a man stand in a bucket and lift himself up by the handle?"

Winston Churchill

title: Have you ever noticed how statists
grift: Have you ever noticed how statists
body:
## "Have you ever noticed how Statists are constantly 'reforming' their own handiwork education reform health care reform welfare reform tax reform the very fact they're always busy 'reforming' is an implicit admission that they didn't get it right the first 50 times"

Lawrence Reed

title: A liberal is someone who feels a great debt
grift: A liberal is someone who feels a great debt
body:
## "A liberal is someone who feels a great debt to his fellow man, which debt he proposes to pay off with your money."

G. Gordon Liddy

title: An unlimited power to tax
grift: An unlimited power to tax
body:
## "An unlimited power to tax involves, necessarily, a power to destroy."

Daniel Webster

title: Giving money and power to government
grift: Giving money and power to government
body:
## "Giving money and power to government is like giving whiskey and car keys to teenage boys."

P.J. O'Rourke

title: No government can exist without taxation
grift: No government can exist without taxation
body:
## "No government can exist without taxation. This money must necessarily be drawn from the pockets of the people."

John Marshall

title: The inherent vice of capitalism
grift: The inherent vice of capitalism
body:
## "The inherent vice of capitalism is the unequal sharing of blessings; the inherent virtue of socialism is the equal sharing of miseries."

Winston Churchill

title: The market is not an invention of capitalism
grift: The market is not an invention of capitalism
body:
## "The market is not an invention of capitalism. It has existed for centuries. It is an invention of civilization."

Mikhail Gorbachev

title: The power to tax involves the power to destroy
grift: The power to tax involves the power to destroy
body:
## "The power to tax involves the power to destroy."

John Marshall

title: You cannot multiply wealth
grift: You cannot multiply wealth
body:
## "You cannot multiply wealth by dividing it."

Adrian Rogers

title: A free people ought not only to be armed
grift: A free people ought not only to be armed
body:
## "A free people ought not only to be armed, but disciplined."

George Washington

title: A government resting on the minority is an aristocracy
grift: A government resting on the minority is an aristocracy
body:
## "A government resting on the minority is an aristocracy, not a democracy."

Thomas Jefferson

title: A people who mean to be their own governors
grift: A people who mean to be their own governors
body:
## "A people who mean to be their own governors must arm themselves with the power knowledge gives."

James Madison

title: A wise and frugal government
grift: A wise and frugal government
body:
## "A wise and frugal government shall restrain men from injuring one another, shall leave them otherwise free to regulate their own pursuits of industry and improvement."

Thomas Jefferson

title: All men having power ought to be distrusted
grift: All men having power ought to be distrusted
body:
## "All men having power ought to be distrusted to a certain degree."

James Madison

title: Democracy is two wolves and a lamb
grift: Democracy is two wolves and a lamb
body:
## "Democracy is two wolves and a lamb voting on what to have for lunch. Liberty is a well-armed lamb contesting the vote."

Benjamin Franklin (attributed)

title: Facts are stubborn things
grift: Facts are stubborn things
body:
## "Facts are stubborn things; and whatever may be our wishes, our inclinations, or the dictates of our passion, they cannot alter the state of facts and evidence."

John Adams

title: Give me liberty or give me death
grift: Give me liberty or give me death
body:
## "Give me liberty, or give me death!"

Patrick Henry

title: Government even in its best state
grift: Government even in its best state
body:
## "Government, even in its best state, is but a necessary evil; in its worst state, an intolerable one."

Thomas Paine

title: Government is best which governs the least
grift: Government is best which governs the least
body:
## "Government is best which governs the least, because its people discipline themselves."

Thomas Jefferson (attributed)

title: Government is not reason; it is not eloquent
grift: Government is not reason; it is not eloquent
body:
## "Government is not reason; it is not eloquent; it is force. Like fire, it is a dangerous servant and a fearful master."

George Washington

title: Guard against the impostures of pretended patriotism
grift: Guard against the impostures of pretended patriotism
body:
## "Guard against the impostures of pretended patriotism."

George Washington

title: He who dares not offend cannot be honest
grift: He who dares not offend cannot be honest
body:
## "He who dares not offend cannot be honest."

Thomas Paine

title: I cannot undertake to lay my finger
grift: I cannot undertake to lay my finger
body:
## "I cannot undertake to lay my finger on that article of the Constitution which granted a right to Congress of expending, on objects of benevolence, the money of their constituents."

James Madison

title: I own that I am not a friend to a very energetic government
grift: I own that I am not a friend to a very energetic government
body:
## "I own that I am not a friend to a very energetic government. It is always oppressive."

Thomas Jefferson

title: I predict future happiness for Americans
grift: I predict future happiness for Americans
body:
## "I predict future happiness for Americans, if they can prevent the government from wasting the labors of the people under the pretense of taking care of them."

Thomas Jefferson

title: I would rather be exposed to the inconveniences
grift: I would rather be exposed to the inconveniences
body:
## "I would rather be exposed to the inconveniences attending too much liberty than those attending too small a degree of it."

Thomas Jefferson

title: If men were angels
grift: If men were angels
body:
## "If men were angels, no government would be necessary."

James Madison

title: If you want to help the poor
grift: If you want to help the poor
body:
## "If you want to help the poor, demonstrate to them how they can help themselves."

Benjamin Franklin

title: It is the duty of the patriot
grift: It is the duty of the patriot
body:
## "It is the duty of the patriot to protect his country from its government."

Thomas Paine

title: Knowledge will forever govern ignorance
grift: Knowledge will forever govern ignorance
body:
## "Knowledge will forever govern ignorance; and a people who mean to be their own governors must arm themselves with the power which knowledge gives."

James Madison

title: Laws that forbid the carrying of arms
grift: Laws that forbid the carrying of arms
body:
## "Laws that forbid the carrying of arms disarm only those who are neither inclined nor determined to commit crimes."

Thomas Jefferson (quoting Cesare Beccaria)

title: Liberty must at all hazards be supported
grift: Liberty must at all hazards be supported
body:
## "Liberty must at all hazards be supported. We have a right to it, derived from our Maker."

John Adams

title: No freeman shall be debarred the use of arms
grift: No freeman shall be debarred the use of arms
body:
## "No freeman shall be debarred the use of arms."

Thomas Jefferson

title: Of all the enemies to public liberty
grift: Of all the enemies to public liberty
body:
## "Of all the enemies to public liberty, war is, perhaps, the most to be dreaded."

James Madison

title: Only a virtuous people are capable of freedom
grift: Only a virtuous people are capable of freedom
body:
## "Only a virtuous people are capable of freedom. As nations become corrupt and vicious, they have more need of masters."

Benjamin Franklin

title: Our Constitution was made only for a moral and religious people
grift: Our Constitution was made only for a moral and religious people
body:
## "Our Constitution was made only for a moral and religious people. It is wholly inadequate to the government of any other."

John Adams

title: Peace, commerce, and honest friendship
grift: Peace, commerce, and honest friendship
body:
## "Peace, commerce, and honest friendship with all nations; entangling alliances with none."

Thomas Jefferson

title: People who are willing to trade freedom for security
grift: People who are willing to trade freedom for security
body:
## "People who are willing to trade freedom for security will end up with neither."

Benjamin Franklin

title: The advancement and diffusion of knowledge
grift: The advancement and diffusion of knowledge
body:
## "The advancement and diffusion of knowledge is the only guardian of true liberty."

James Madison

title: The Constitution is not a suicide pact
grift: The Constitution is not a suicide pact
body:
## "The Constitution of the United States is a law for rulers and people, equally in war and in peace, and covers with the shield of its protection all classes of men, at all times and under all circumstances."

David Davis

title: The Constitution is not an instrument for the government
grift: The Constitution is not an instrument for the government
body:
## "The Constitution is not an instrument for the government to restrain the people, it is an instrument for the people to restrain the government."

Patrick Henry

title: The Constitution only gives people the right to pursue happiness
grift: The Constitution only gives people the right to pursue happiness
body:
## "The Constitution only gives people the right to pursue happiness. You have to catch it yourself."

Benjamin Franklin

title: The Constitution shall never be construed
grift: The Constitution shall never be construed
body:
## "The Constitution shall never be construed to prevent the people of the United States who are peaceable citizens from keeping their own arms."

Samuel Adams

title: The danger in democracy is not tyranny by the minority
grift: The danger in democracy is not tyranny by the minority
body:
## "The danger in democracy is not tyranny by the minority but tyranny by the majority."

James Madison

title: The first duty of society is justice
grift: The first duty of society is justice
body:
## "The first duty of society is justice."

Alexander Hamilton

title: The great danger in republics
grift: The great danger in republics
body:
## "The great danger in republics is that the majority may not sufficiently respect the rights of the minority."

James Madison

title: The legitimate powers of government extend
grift: The legitimate powers of government extend
body:
## "The legitimate powers of government extend to such acts only as are injurious to others."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The liberties of a people never were
grift: The liberties of a people never were
body:
## "The liberties of a people never were, nor ever will be, secure when the transactions of their rulers may be concealed from them."

Patrick Henry

title: The man who reads nothing
grift: The man who reads nothing
body:
## "The man who reads nothing at all is better educated than the man who reads nothing but newspapers."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The man who trades freedom for security
grift: The man who trades freedom for security
body:
## "The man who trades freedom for security does not deserve nor will he ever receive either."

Benjamin Franklin

title: The natural progress of things is for liberty to yield
grift: The natural progress of things is for liberty to yield
body:
## "The natural progress of things is for liberty to yield and government to gain ground."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The people are the only sure reliance
grift: The people are the only sure reliance
body:
## "The people are the only sure reliance for the preservation of our liberty."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The power under the Constitution will always be in the people
grift: The power under the Constitution will always be in the people
body:
## "The power under the Constitution will always be in the people."

George Washington

title: The price of freedom is eternal vigilance
grift: The price of freedom is eternal vigilance
body:
## "The price of freedom is eternal vigilance."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The purpose of a constitution
grift: The purpose of a constitution
body:
## "The purpose of a constitution is to restrain the government, not the people."

Patrick Henry

title: The rights of individuals should be the primary object
grift: The rights of individuals should be the primary object
body:
## "The rights of individuals should be the primary object of all governments."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The spirit of resistance to government
grift: The spirit of resistance to government
body:
## "The spirit of resistance to government is so valuable on certain occasions that I wish it to be always kept alive."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The tree of liberty must be refreshed
grift: The tree of liberty must be refreshed
body:
## "The tree of liberty must be refreshed from time to time with the blood of patriots and tyrants."

Thomas Jefferson

title: The ultimate authority resides in the people alone
grift: The ultimate authority resides in the people alone
body:
## "The ultimate authority resides in the people alone."

James Madison

title: Those who expect to reap the blessings of freedom
grift: Those who expect to reap the blessings of freedom
body:
## "Those who expect to reap the blessings of freedom must, like men, undergo the fatigue of supporting it."

Thomas Paine

title: Those who would give up essential liberty
grift: Those who would give up essential liberty
body:
## "Those who would give up essential liberty to purchase a little temporary safety deserve neither liberty nor safety."

Benjamin Franklin

title: To compel a man to furnish contributions
grift: To compel a man to furnish contributions
body:
## "To compel a man to furnish contributions of money for the propagation of opinions which he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

Thomas Jefferson

title: To compel a man to furnish funds for the propagation of ideas he disbelieves
grift: To compel a man to furnish funds for the propagation of ideas he disbelieves
body:
## "To compel a man to furnish funds for the propagation of ideas he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

Thomas Jefferson

title: To compel a man to subsidize
grift: To compel a man to subsidize
body:
## "To compel a man to subsidize the teaching of ideas he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical."

Thomas Jefferson

title: Tyranny is defined as that which is legal for the government
grift: Tyranny is defined as that which is legal for the government
body:
## "Tyranny is defined as that which is legal for the government but illegal for the citizenry."

Thomas Jefferson

title: We are not to expect to be transported from despotism to liberty
grift: We are not to expect to be transported from despotism to liberty
body:
## "We are not to expect to be transported from despotism to liberty in a featherbed."

Thomas Jefferson

title: When injustice becomes law
grift: When injustice becomes law
body:
## "When injustice becomes law, resistance becomes duty."

Thomas Jefferson

title: When people fear the government, there is tyranny
grift: When people fear the government, there is tyranny
body:
## "When people fear the government, there is tyranny; when the government fears the people, there is liberty."

Thomas Jefferson

title: When the people fear the government
grift: When the people fear the government
body:
## "When the people fear the government, there is tyranny; when the government fears the people, there is liberty."

Thomas Jefferson

title: When the people find they can vote themselves money
grift: When the people find they can vote themselves money
body:
## "When the people find they can vote themselves money, that will herald the end of the republic."

Benjamin Franklin (attributed)

title: Without liberty, law loses its nature
grift: Without liberty, law loses its nature
body:
## "Without liberty, law loses its nature and justice loses its meaning."

Alexander Hamilton

title: The dark night of fascism is always descending
grift: The dark night of fascism is always descending
body:
## "The dark night of fascism is always descending in the United States and yet lands only in Europe."

Tom Wolfe

title: Socialism irons out the boom-bust cycle
grift: Socialism irons out the boom-bust cycle
body:
## "Socialism irons out the boom-bust cycle by eliminating the boom part."

Lawrence W. Reed

title: The state is that great fiction
grift: The state is that great fiction
body:
## "The state is that great fiction by which everyone tries to live at the expense of everyone else."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: When buying and selling are controlled by legislation
grift: When buying and selling are controlled by legislation
body:
## "When buying and selling are controlled by legislation, the first things to be bought and sold are legislators."

P.J. O'Rourke

title: A government big enough to give you everything you want
grift: A government big enough to give you everything you want
body:
## "A government big enough to give you everything you want is a government big enough to take from you everything you have."

Gerald R. Ford

title: A government that robs Peter
grift: A government that robs Peter
body:
## "A government that robs Peter to pay Paul can always depend on the support of Paul."

George Bernard Shaw

title: A man's admiration for absolute government
grift: A man's admiration for absolute government
body:
## "A man's admiration for absolute government is proportionate to the contempt he feels for those around him."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: A state that dwarfs its men
grift: A state that dwarfs its men
body:
## "A state that dwarfs its men, in order that they may be more docile instruments in its hands even for beneficial purposes, will find that with small men no great thing can really be accomplished."

John Stuart Mill

title: Democracy must be something more
grift: Democracy must be something more
body:
## "Democracy must be something more than two wolves and a sheep voting on what to have for dinner."

James Bovard

title: Every step we take towards making the State our Caretaker
grift: Every step we take towards making the State our Caretaker
body:
## "Every step we take towards making the State our Caretaker of our lives, by that much we move toward making the State our Master."

Dwight D. Eisenhower

title: Everyone wants to live at the expense of the state
grift: Everyone wants to live at the expense of the state
body:
## "Everyone wants to live at the expense of the state. They forget that the state lives at the expense of everyone."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: Government has no other end
grift: Government has no other end
body:
## "Government has no other end, but the preservation of property."

John Locke

title: Government is the great fiction
grift: Government is the great fiction
body:
## "Government is the great fiction through which everybody endeavors to live at the expense of everybody else."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: If you have ten thousand regulations
grift: If you have ten thousand regulations
body:
## "If you have ten thousand regulations, you destroy all respect for the law."

Winston Churchill

title: In matters of conscience, the law of the majority has no place
grift: In matters of conscience, the law of the majority has no place
body:
## "In matters of conscience, the law of the majority has no place."

Mahatma Gandhi

title: It is not wisdom but Authority that makes a law
grift: It is not wisdom but Authority that makes a law
body:
## "It is not wisdom but Authority that makes a law."

Thomas Hobbes

title: Let us not seek the Republican answer or the Democratic answer
grift: Let us not seek the Republican answer or the Democratic answer
body:
## "Let us not seek the Republican answer or the Democratic answer, but the right answer."

John F. Kennedy

title: Man is not made for the state
grift: Man is not made for the state
body:
## "Man is not made for the state. The state is made for man."

Albert Einstein

title: That government is best which governs least
grift: That government is best which governs least
body:
## "That government is best which governs least."

Henry David Thoreau

title: The bigger the government, the more it can give you
grift: The bigger the government, the more it can give you
body:
## "The bigger the government, the more it can give you everything you want — and take away everything you have."

Anonymous Conservative Axiom

title: The bigger the government, the smaller the citizen
grift: The bigger the government, the smaller the citizen
body:
## "The bigger the government, the smaller the citizen."

Dennis Prager

title: The Constitution is not neutral
grift: The Constitution is not neutral
body:
## "The Constitution is not neutral. It was designed to take the government off the backs of people."

William O. Douglas

title: The difference between a welfare state and a totalitarian state
grift: The difference between a welfare state and a totalitarian state
body:
## "The difference between a welfare state and a totalitarian state is a matter of time."

Ayn Rand

title: The government was set to protect man
grift: The government was set to protect man
body:
## "The government was set to protect man from criminals and the Constitution was written to protect man from the government."

Ayn Rand

title: The more corrupt the state, the more numerous the laws
grift: The more corrupt the state, the more numerous the laws
body:
## "The more corrupt the state, the more numerous the laws."

Tacitus

title: The more laws, the less justice
grift: The more laws, the less justice
body:
## "The more laws, the less justice."

Marcus Tullius Cicero

title: The most dangerous man to any government
grift: The most dangerous man to any government
body:
## "The most dangerous man to any government is the man who is able to think things out for himself."

H.L. Mencken

title: The philosophy of the schoolroom in one generation
grift: The philosophy of the schoolroom in one generation
body:
## "The philosophy of the schoolroom in one generation will be the philosophy of government in the next."

Abraham Lincoln

title: The state is a servant of the people
grift: The state is a servant of the people
body:
## "The state is a servant of the people. It must never become an absolute master."

Vaclav Havel

title: There are no necessary evils in government
grift: There are no necessary evils in government
body:
## "There are no necessary evils in government. Its evils exist only in its abuses."

Andrew Jackson

title: What is not just is not law
grift: What is not just is not law
body:
## "What is not just is not law."

William Lloyd Garrison

title: You can’t legislate morality
grift: You can’t legislate morality
body:
## "You can’t legislate morality. You can only regulate behavior."

Clarence Thomas

title: A government which lays taxes on the people
grift: A government which lays taxes on the people
body:
## "A government which lays taxes on the people not required by urgent public necessity and sound public policy is not a protector of liberty but an instrument of tyranny it condemns the citizen to servitude"

Calvin Coolidge

title: The greatest dangers to liberty lurk in insidious encroachment
grift: The greatest dangers to liberty lurk in insidious encroachment
body:
## "The greatest dangers to liberty lurk in insidious encroachment by men of zeal, well-meaning but without understanding."

Louis D. Brandeis

title: Human beings are born with different capacities
grift: Human beings are born with different capacities
body:
## "Human beings are born with different capacities, if they are free, they are not equal. And it they are equal, they are not free."

Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn

title: Liberty is not a means to a higher political end
grift: Liberty is not a means to a higher political end
body:
## "Liberty is not a means to a higher political end. It is itself the highest political end."

Lord Acton

title: Liberty means responsibility
grift: Liberty means responsibility
body:
## "Liberty means responsibility. That is why most men dread it."

George Bernard Shaw

title: Over himself, over his own body and mind
grift: Over himself, over his own body and mind
body:
## "Over himself, over his own body and mind, the individual is sovereign."

John Stuart Mill

title: The end of law is not to abolish or restrain
grift: The end of law is not to abolish or restrain
body:
## "The end of law is not to abolish or restrain, but to preserve and enlarge freedom."

John Locke

title: A free press stands as one of the great interpreters
grift: A free press stands as one of the great interpreters
body:
## "A free press stands as one of the great interpreters between the government and the people."

Herbert Hoover

title: Civilization is the process of setting man free
grift: Civilization is the process of setting man free
body:
## "Civilization is the process of setting man free from men."

Ayn Rand

title: Eternal vigilance is the price of liberty
grift: Eternal vigilance is the price of liberty
body:
## "Eternal vigilance is the price of liberty."

Wendell Phillips

title: Experience should teach us to be most on our guard
grift: Experience should teach us to be most on our guard
body:
## "Experience should teach us to be most on our guard to protect liberty when the government's purposes are beneficent."

Louis Brandeis

title: Freedom is never voluntarily given by the oppressor
grift: Freedom is never voluntarily given by the oppressor
body:
## "Freedom is never voluntarily given by the oppressor; it must be demanded by the oppressed."

Martin Luther King Jr.

title: It is seldom that liberty of any kind is lost all at once
grift: It is seldom that liberty of any kind is lost all at once
body:
## "It is seldom that liberty of any kind is lost all at once."

David Hume

title: Liberty and independence forever
grift: Liberty and independence forever
body:
## "Liberty and independence forever."

Davey Crockett

title: Liberty is always dangerous
grift: Liberty is always dangerous
body:
## "Liberty is always dangerous, but it is the safest thing we have."

Harry Emerson Fosdick

title: Liberty is being able to engage
grift: Liberty is being able to engage
body:
## "Liberty is being able to engage in peaceable voluntary exchange without interference from others."

Walter E Williams

title: Liberty is not a means to a political end
grift: Liberty is not a means to a political end
body:
## "Liberty is not a means to a political end. It is itself the highest political end."

Lord Acton

title: Liberty is not collective, it is personal
grift: Liberty is not collective, it is personal
body:
## "Liberty is not collective, it is personal. All liberty is individual liberty."

Calvin Coolidge

title: Liberty is not the power of doing what we like
grift: Liberty is not the power of doing what we like
body:
## "Liberty is not the power of doing what we like, but the right of being able to do what we ought."

Lord Acton

title: No man's life, liberty or property is safe
grift: No man's life, liberty or property is safe
body:
## "No man's life, liberty or property is safe while the legislature is in session."

Gideon J. Tucker (attributed to Mark Twain)

title: None are more hopelessly enslaved
grift: None are more hopelessly enslaved
body:
## "None are more hopelessly enslaved than those who falsely believe they are free."

Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

title: None can love freedom heartily
grift: None can love freedom heartily
body:
## "None can love freedom heartily but good men; the rest love not freedom but license."

John Milton

title: Nothing is more destructive of individual character
grift: Nothing is more destructive of individual character
body:
## "Nothing is more destructive of individual character than being raised in dependence on the government."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: The essence of freedom
grift: The essence of freedom
body:
## "The essence of freedom is the proper limitation of government."

Founding Fathers

title: The history of liberty is a history of resistance
grift: The history of liberty is a history of resistance
body:
## "The history of liberty is a history of resistance."

Woodrow Wilson

title: The issue is always the same
grift: The issue is always the same
body:
## "The issue is always the same: the individual versus the collective."

Ayn Rand

title: The legitimate object of government
grift: The legitimate object of government
body:
## "The legitimate object of government is to do for a community of people whatever they need to have done but cannot do at all in their separate and individual capacities."

Abraham Lincoln

title: The liberty of the individual must be thus far limited
grift: The liberty of the individual must be thus far limited
body:
## "The liberty of the individual must be thus far limited; he must not make himself a nuisance to other people."

John Stuart Mill

title: The natural liberty of man
grift: The natural liberty of man
body:
## "The natural liberty of man is to be free from any superior power on Earth."

Samuel Adams

title: The only freedom which deserves the name
grift: The only freedom which deserves the name
body:
## "The only freedom which deserves the name is that of pursuing our own good in our own way."

John Stuart Mill

title: The only thing that saves us from bureaucracy
grift: The only thing that saves us from bureaucracy
body:
## "The only thing that saves us from the bureaucracy is inefficiency. An efficient bureaucracy is the greatest threat to liberty."

Eugene McCarthy

title: The progress of society depends not on legislation
grift: The progress of society depends not on legislation
body:
## "The progress of society depends not on legislation, but on the moral sense of individuals."

William E. Gladstone

title: The reason every Marxist government
grift: The reason every Marxist government
body:
## "The reason every Marxist government in the history of the world turned massively repressive is not because they all had the misfortune of being hijacked by murderous thugs. It's that the ideology itself prioritizes class justice over individual rights and makes no allowance for legitimate disagreement."

Jonathan Chait

title: The right to be let alone
grift: The right to be let alone
body:
## "The right to be let alone is the most comprehensive of rights, and the right most valued by a free people."

Louis Brandeis

title: The smallest minority on earth is the individual
grift: The smallest minority on earth is the individual
body:
## "The smallest minority on earth is the individual. Those who deny individual rights cannot claim to be defenders of minorities."

Ayn Rand

title: The true danger is when liberty is nibbled away
grift: The true danger is when liberty is nibbled away
body:
## "The true danger is when liberty is nibbled away, for expedients, and by parts."

Edmund Burke

title: The will of the people is not always the law
grift: The will of the people is not always the law
body:
## "The will of the people is not always the law. The law should protect the rights of the individual, even against the majority."

Barry Goldwater

title: There is no liberty without independence of mind
grift: There is no liberty without independence of mind
body:
## "There is no liberty without independence of mind."

Alexis de Tocqueville

title: When we lose our individual independence
grift: When we lose our individual independence
body:
## "When we lose our individual independence in the corporateness of a mass movement, we find a new freedom — freedom to hate, bully, lie, torture, murder and betray without shame and remorse."

Eric Hoffer

title: All socialism involves slavery
grift: All socialism involves slavery
body:
## "All socialism involves slavery."

Herbert Spencer

title: Every collectivist revolution rides in on a Trojan horse of 'Emergency'
grift: Every collectivist revolution rides in on a Trojan horse of 'Emergency'
body:
## "Every collectivist revolution rides in on a Trojan horse of 'Emergency'."

Herbert Hoover

title: Socialism is a philosophy of failure
grift: Socialism is a philosophy of failure
body:
## "Socialism is a philosophy of failure, the creed of ignorance, and the gospel of envy."

Winston Churchill

title: The goal of socialism is communism
grift: The goal of socialism is communism
body:
## "The goal of socialism is communism."

Vladimir Lenin

title: If you want to know who controls you
