grift: If you want to know who controls you
body:
## "If you want to know who controls you, look at who you are not allowed to criticize."

Voltaire

title: Islam's borders are bloody and so are its innards
grift: Islam's borders are bloody and so are its innards
body:
## "Islam's borders are bloody and so are its innards. The fundamental problem for the West is not Islamic fundamentalism. It is Islam, a different civilisation whose people are convinced of the superiority of their culture and are obsessed with the inferiority of their power."

Samuel P. Huntington

title: To act on the belief that we possess
grift: To act on the belief that we possess
body:
## "To act on the belief that we possess the knowledge and the power which enable us to shape the processes of society entirely to our liking, knowledge which in fact we do not possess, is likely to make us do much harm."

Fredrich Hayek

title: Good intentions will always be pleaded
grift: Good intentions will always be pleaded
body:
## "Good intentions will always be pleaded for every assumption of authority. It is hardly too strong to say that the Constitution was made to guard the people against the dangers of good intentions."

Daniel Webster

title: Of all tyrannies, a tyranny sincerely exercised for the good of its victims
grift: Of all tyrannies, a tyranny sincerely exercised for the good of its victims
body:
## "Of all tyrannies, a tyranny sincerely exercised for the good of its victims may be the most oppressive."

C.S. Lewis

title: Political correctness is tyranny
grift: Political correctness is tyranny
body:
## "Political correctness is tyranny with manners."

Charlton Heston

title: Power tends to corrupt
grift: Power tends to corrupt
body:
## "Power tends to corrupt, and absolute power corrupts absolutely."

Lord Acton

title: The evils of tyranny are rarely seen
grift: The evils of tyranny are rarely seen
body:
## "The evils of tyranny are rarely seen but by him who resists it."

John Hay

title: The only purpose for which power can be rightfully exercised
grift: The only purpose for which power can be rightfully exercised
body:
## "The only purpose for which power can be rightfully exercised over any member of a civilized community, against his will, is to prevent harm to others."

John Stuart Mill

title: The welfare of humanity is always the alibi of tyrants
grift: The welfare of humanity is always the alibi of tyrants
body:
## "The welfare of humanity is always the alibi of tyrants."

Albert Camus

title: There is no worse tyranny
grift: There is no worse tyranny
body:
## "There is no worse tyranny than to force a man to pay for what he does not want merely because you think it would be good for him."

Robert A. Heinlein

title: I always think it's a sign of victory
grift: I always think it's a sign of victory
body:
## "I always think it's a sign of victory when they move on to the ad hominem."

Christopher Hitchens

title: If you want to change society, you have to change the metaphor
grift: If you want to change society, you have to change the metaphor
body:
## "If you want to change society, you have to change the metaphor."

George Lakoff

title: It is easier to build strong children than to repair broken men
grift: It is easier to build strong children than to repair broken men
body:
## "It is easier to build strong children than to repair broken men."

Frederick Douglass

title: It is not from the benevolence of the butcher
grift: It is not from the benevolence of the butcher
body:
## "It is not from the benevolence of the butcher, the brewer, or the baker that we expect our dinner, but from their regard to their own interest."

Adam Smith

title: Religion is regarded by the common people
grift: Religion is regarded by the common people
body:
## "Religion is regarded by the common people as true, by the wise as false, and by rulers as useful."

Seneca

title: The life of man is of no greater duration than the breath of his nostrils
grift: The life of man is of no greater duration than the breath of his nostrils
body:
## "The life of man is of no greater duration than the breath of his nostrils."

Plato

title: The question is not who is going to let me
grift: The question is not who is going to let me
body:
## "The question isn't who is going to let me; it's who is going to stop me."

Ayn Rand

title: The universe is not only stranger than we imagine
grift: The universe is not only stranger than we imagine
body:
## "The universe is not only stranger than we imagine, but stranger than we can imagine."

Paraphrasing JBS Haldane

title: There's a word for people who are kept
grift: There's a word for people who are kept
body:
## "There's a word for people who are kept safe fed clothed housed and sustained fully by others... and that word is slaves."

Bill Whittle

title: We know they are lying. They know they are lying
grift: We know they are lying. They know they are lying
body:
## "We know they are lying. They know they are lying. They know that we know they are lying. We know that they know that we know they are lying. And still they continue to lie."

Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn

title: Young people are told at university
grift: Young people are told at university
body:
## "Young people are told at university that they come from nowhere and belong to nothing: that all pre-existing forms of membership are null and void. They are offered a rite of passage into cultural nothingness, since this is the only way to achieve the egalitarian goal."

Roger Scruton

title: ~'A leftist is someone who feels a great debt'
grift: ~'A leftist is someone who feels a great debt'
body:
## ~"A leftist is someone who feels a great debt to his fellow man, which debt he proposes to pay off with your money."

G. Gordon Liddy

title: A 'liberal' is a man too broadminded
grift: A 'liberal' is a man too broadminded
body:
## "A 'liberal' is a man too broadminded to take his own side in a quarrel."

Robert Frost

title: A man is no less a slave
grift: A man is no less a slave
body:
## "A man is no less a slave because he is allowed to choose a new master once in a term of years."

Lysander Spooner

title: A society that has no children has no future
grift: A society that has no children has no future
body:
## "A society that has no children has no future. The Western world is contracepting itself out of existence, and the vacuum will be filled by others."

Mark Steyn

title: All that is necessary for evil to triumph
grift: All that is necessary for evil to triumph
body:
## "All that is necessary for evil to triumph is for good men to do nothing."

Edmund Burke

title: As Hayek himself understood
grift: As Hayek himself understood
body:
## "As Hayek himself understood, however, the case for freedom and free markets must continually be rejuvenated and made again and again and again. The project is never completed, as more recent political developments in Britain and the United States attest. Opposing ideas—those of collectivism of one form or another—are always being generated, refined, and spread. Failure by classical liberals and other defenders of a society based on free markets and strictly limited government to counter these collectivist ideas will guarantee the victory of collectivism."

Donald J. Boudreaux

title: Big Government is the small option
grift: Big Government is the small option
body:
## "Big Government is the small option: it's the guarantee of smaller freedom, smaller homes, smaller cars, smaller opportunities, smaller lives."

Mark Steyn

title: Chesterton's Fence: In the matter of reforming things
grift: Chesterton's Fence: In the matter of reforming things
body:
## Chesterton's Fence: "In the matter of reforming things, as distinct from deforming them, there is one plain and simple principle; a principle which will probably be called a paradox. There exists in such a case a certain institution or law; let us say, for the sake of simplicity, a fence or gate erected across a road. The more modern type of reformer goes gaily up to it and says, 'I don't see the use of this; let us clear it away.' To which the more intelligent type of reformer will do well to answer: 'If you don't see the use of it, I certainly won't let you clear it away. Go away and think. Then, when you can come back and tell me that you do see the use of it, I may allow you to destroy it.'"

G.K. Chesterton

title: Demography is Destiny
grift: Demography is Destiny
body:
## "Demography is destiny. When your birth rate collapses, you're not just shrinking your population—you're shrinking your culture, your values, your way of life."

Mark Steyn, 2012

title: Don't expect to build up the weak
grift: Don't expect to build up the weak
body:
## "Don't expect to build up the weak by pulling down the strong."

Calvin Coolidge

title: Don't fix what ain't broke.
grift: Don't fix what ain't broke.
body:
## "Don't fix what ain't broke."

Anonymous

title: Each of us has a natural right, from God
grift: Each of us has a natural right, from God
body:
## "Each of us has a natural right, from God, to defend his person, his liberty, and his property."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: Europe's problem is not that it has too many people
grift: Europe's problem is not that it has too many people
body:
## "Europe's problem is not that it has too many people but that it has too few babies. Sub-replacement fertility rates mean that, within a couple of generations, the Europe we know will be gone, replaced by a very different demographic reality."

Mark Steyn, America Alone

title: Every man has a property in his own person
grift: Every man has a property in his own person
body:
## "Every man has a property in his own person. This nobody has a right to, but himself."

John Locke

title: Everything that is really great
grift: Everything that is really great
body:
## "Everything that is really great and inspiring is created by the individual who can labour in freedom."

Albert Einstein

title: Extremism in the defense of liberty
grift: Extremism in the defense of liberty
body:
## "Extremism in the defense of liberty is no vice. And moderation in the pursuit of justice is no virtue.

Barry Goldwater

title: For Al Gore and Paul Ehrlich and Co
grift: For Al Gore and Paul Ehrlich and Co
body:
## "For Al Gore and Paul Ehrlich and Co., whatever the problem, the solution is always the same. Whether it's global cooling, global warming, or overpopulation, we need bigger government, more regulation, higher taxes, and a massive transfer of power from the citizens to some unelected self-perpetuating crisis lobby."

Mark Steyn

title: Freedom is messy. In free societies, people will fall
grift: Freedom is messy. In free societies, people will fall
body:
## "Freedom is messy. In free societies, people will fall through the cracks - drink too much, eat too much, buy unaffordable homes, fail to make prudent provision for health care, and much else. But the price of being relieved of all those tiresome choices by a benign paternal government is far too high."

Mark Steyn

title: Government does not solve problems
grift: Government does not solve problems
body:
## "Government does not solve problems; it subsidizes them."

Ronald Reagan

title: Government is a broker in pillage
grift: Government is a broker in pillage
body:
## "Government is a broker in pillage, and every election is a sort of advanced auction on stolen goods."

H L Mencken

title: He who opens a school door, closes a prison
grift: He who opens a school door, closes a prison
body:
## "He who opens a school door, closes a prison."

Victor Hugo

title: If all cultures are equal
grift: If all cultures are equal
body:
## "If all cultures are equal, then cannibalism is just a matter of culinary taste"

Leo Strauss

title: If I can't trust you not to put a boy
grift: If I can't trust you not to put a boy
body:
## "If I can't trust you not to put a boy in a teenage girl's locker room, how will I ever listen to your plan for taxes and the economy?... I will not, because l've already concluded you're a lunatic."

Scott Jennings

title: If liberty means anything
grift: If liberty means anything
body:
## "If liberty means anything at all, it means the right to tell people what they do not want to hear. If liberty means anything at all, it means the right to tell people what they do not want to hear them."

George Orwell

title: If the natural tendencies of mankind
grift: If the natural tendencies of mankind
body:
## "If the natural tendencies of mankind are so bad that it is not safe to permit people to be free, how is it that the tendencies of these organizers are always good? Do not the legislators and their appointed agents also belong to the human race? Or do they believe that they themselves are made of a finer clay than the rest of mankind?"

Frédéric Bastiat

title: If you're a descendant of a race of people
grift: If you're a descendant of a race of people
body:
## "If you're a descendant of a race of people who explored the known world, invented 95% of modern technology, civilized half the planet, and generated most of the philosophical ideas on which civilisation has been built, but you're ashamed of yourself, then you might be brainwashed."

Anon

title: If your schoolhouse is crumbling
grift: If your schoolhouse is crumbling
body:
## "If your schoolhouse is crumbling, your hospitals are overwhelmed, and your pension plans are bankrupt, it's not because of 'overpopulation' but because of underpopulation — too few people to sustain the systems we've built."

Mark Steyn

title: In politics, stupidity is not a handicap
grift: In politics, stupidity is not a handicap
body:
## "In politics, stupidity is not a handicap."

Napoleon Bonaparte

title: Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere
grift: Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere
body:
## "Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere."

Martin Luther King Jr.

title: It is difficult to free fools
grift: It is difficult to free fools
body:
## "It is difficult to free fools from the chains they revere."

Voltaire

title: It is with infinite caution that any man
grift: It is with infinite caution that any man
body:
## "It is with infinite caution that any man ought to venture upon pulling down an edifice which has answered in any tolerable degree for ages the common purposes of society, or on building it up again, without having models and patterns of approved utility before his eyes."

Edmund Burke

title: Jokes are one of the things that bind the culture
grift: Jokes are one of the things that bind the culture
body:
## "Jokes are one of the things that bind the culture. If you can't have jokes about everybody in society and if one group is hedged off and protected then that group can never truly be integrated."

Mark Steyn

title: Let me offer you my definition of social justice
grift: Let me offer you my definition of social justice
body:
## "Let me offer you my definition of social justice: I keep what I earn and you keep what you earn. Do you disagree? Well then tell me how much of what I earn belongs to you - and why?."

Walter E Williams

title: Love your country, but never
grift: Love your country, but never
body:
## "Love your country, but never trust your government."

Robert Novak

title: My definition of a free society
grift: My definition of a free society
body:
## "My definition of a free society is a society where it is safe to be unpopular."

Adlai Stevenson

title: Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful citizens
grift: Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful citizens
body:
## "Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful, committed citizens can change the world. Indeed, it is the only thing that ever has."

Margaret Mead

title: Never let a crisis go to waste
grift: Never let a crisis go to waste
body:
## "Never let a crisis go to waste. They are opportunities to do big things."

Rahm Emanuel

title: No society can surely be flourishing
grift: No society can surely be flourishing
body:
## "No society can surely be flourishing and happy, of which the far greater part of the members are poor and miserable."

Adam Smith

title: None can love freedom heartily, but good men
grift: None can love freedom heartily, but good men
body:
## "None can love freedom heartily, but good men; the rest love not freedom, but licence himself."

John Locke

title: Not all Muslims support terrorists
grift: Not all Muslims support terrorists
body:
## "Not all Muslims support terrorists — though enough of them share their basic objectives (the wish to live under Islamic law in Europe and North America) to function wittingly or otherwise as the 'good cop' end of an Islamic good cop/bad cop routine."

Mark Steyn

title: Once you get a taste for shutting people up
grift: Once you get a taste for shutting people up
body:
## "Once you get a taste for shutting people up, it's hard to stop. Why bother winning the debate when it's easier to close it down?"

Mark Steyn

title: Progressivism was a sister movement of fascism
grift: Progressivism was a sister movement of fascism
body:
## "Progressivism was a sister movement of fascism, and today's 'liberalism' is the daughter of Progressivism."

Jonah Goldberg

title: Scratch an intellectual and you'll find
grift: Scratch an intellectual and you'll find
body:
## "Scratch an intellectual and you'll find a would-be aristocrat who loathes the sight, the sight and the smell of common folk."

Eric Hoffer

title: Show me a young Conservative and
grift: Show me a young Conservative and
body:
## "Show me a young Conservative and I'll show you someone with no heart. Show me an old Liberal and I'll show you someone with no brains.

Winston Churchill

title: So much of liberalism in its classical sense
grift: So much of liberalism in its classical sense
body:
## "So much of liberalism in its classical sense is taken for granted in the west today and even disrespected. We take freedom for granted, and because of this we don't understand how incredibly vulnerable it is."

Niall Ferguson

title: Teaching Latin and Greek in high school
grift: Teaching Latin and Greek in high school
body:
## "In 100 years we have gone from teaching Latin and Greek in high school to teaching Remedial English in college."

Joseph Sobran

title: The burden of proof lies on the one
grift: The burden of proof lies on the one
body:
## "The burden of proof lies on the one who wishes to change what has been tried and tested, not on the one who wishes to preserve it."

Roger Scruton

title: The danger is not that a particular class is unfit to govern
grift: The danger is not that a particular class is unfit to govern
body:
## "The danger is not that a particular class is unfit to govern. Every class is unfit to govern."

Lord Acton

title: The great aim of education is not knowledge but action
grift: The great aim of education is not knowledge but action
body:
## "The great aim of education is not knowledge but action."

Herbert Spencer

title: The hardest thing to explain is
grift: The hardest thing to explain is
body:
## "The hardest thing to explain is the glaringly evident which everybody has decided not to see."

Ayn Rand

title: The larger the government, the smaller the citizen
grift: The larger the government, the smaller the citizen
body:
## "The larger the government, the smaller the citizen."

Dennis Prager

title: The Left doesn't want to win the debate
grift: The Left doesn't want to win the debate
body:
## "The Left doesn't want to win the debate, they want to cancel the debate"

Mark Steyn

title: The liberties of a people
grift: The liberties of a people
body:
## "The liberties of a people will never be secure, when the transactions of their rulers may be concealed from them."

Patrick Henry

title: The man who is not allowed to own property
grift: The man who is not allowed to own property
body:
## "The man who is not allowed to own property is a man reduced to dependence."

John Locke

title: The moderate left are following the same
grift: The moderate left are following the same
body:
## "The moderate left are following the same character as the 'moderate Muslim': unlike their hardcore brethren they don't want to decapitate you themselves, but they do enjoy watching."

Mark Steyn

title: The most harm of all is done
grift: The most harm of all is done
body:
## "The most harm of all is done when power is in the hands of people who are absolutely persuaded of the purity of their intentions"

Milton Friedman

title: The politician attempts to remedy the evil
grift: The politician attempts to remedy the evil
body:
## "The politician attempts to remedy the evil by increasing the very thing that caused he evil in the first place: legal plunder."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: The problem isn't that Johnny can't read
grift: The problem isn't that Johnny can't read
body:
## "The problem isn't that Johnny can't read. The problem isn't even that Johnny can't think. The problem is that Johnny doesn't know what thinking is."

Richard Mitchell

title: The secret to happiness is freedom
grift: The secret to happiness is freedom
body:
## "The secret to happiness is freedom... And the secret to freedom is courage."

Thucydides

title: The ultimate result of shielding men from the effects of folly
grift: The ultimate result of shielding men from the effects of folly
body:
## "The ultimate result of shielding men from the effects of folly is to fill the world with fools."

Herbert Spencer

title: The urge to save humanity
grift: The urge to save humanity
body:
## "The urge to save humanity is almost always a false front for the urge to rule."

H.L. Mencken

title: The West's fertility rate
grift: The West's fertility rate
body:
## "The West's fertility rate is not just below replacement level; it's in a death spiral. Japan, Germany, Italy — they're not reproducing enough to maintain their populations, let alone their economies or cultures."

Mark Steyn, from a 2010 interview with Hugh Hewitt.

title: There's no fine line between 'free speech' and 'hate speech
grift: There's no fine line between 'free speech' and 'hate speech
body:
## "There's no fine line between 'free speech' and 'hate speech': Free speech is hate speech; it's for the speech you hate—and for all your speech that the other guy hates. If you don't have free speech, then you can't have an honest discussion."

Mark Steyn

title: To educate a man in mind and not in morals
grift: To educate a man in mind and not in morals
body:
## "To educate a man in mind and not in morals is to educate a menace to society."

Theodore Roosevelt

title: To make men love their country, their country ought to be lovely
grift: To make men love their country, their country ought to be lovely
body:
## "To make men love their country, their country ought to be lovely."

Edmund Burke

title: To sin by silence when they should protest
grift: To sin by silence when they should protest
body:
## "To sin by silence when they should protest makes cowards of men."

Ella Wheeler Wilcox

title: Truth is treason in the empire of lies
grift: Truth is treason in the empire of lies
body:
## "Truth is treason in the empire of lies."

Ron Paul

title: Unlike classical liberalism, which saw the government as
grift: Unlike classical liberalism, which saw the government as
body:
## "Unlike classical liberalism, which saw the government as a necessary evil, or simply a benign but voluntary social contract for free men to enter into willingly, the belief that the entire society was one organic whole left no room for those who didn't want to behave, let alone 'evolve'".

Jonah Goldberg

title: We cannot doubt that self-interest is the mainspring
grift: We cannot doubt that self-interest is the mainspring
body:
## "We cannot doubt that self-interest is the mainspring of human nature. It must be clearly understood that this word is used here to designate a universal, incontestable fact, resulting from the nature of man, and not an adverse judgment, as would be the word selfishness."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: We must not regard every change as an improvement
grift: We must not regard every change as an improvement
body:
## "We must not regard every change as an improvement, nor must we think that what is ancient is necessarily good; but we should examine all things and retain what is good."

Aristotle

title: What is prudence in the conduct of every private family
grift: What is prudence in the conduct of every private family
body:
## "What is prudence in the conduct of every private family can scarce be folly in that of a great kingdom."

Adam Smith

title: What's worse than debauching your finances?
grift: What's worse than debauching your finances?
body:
## "What's worse than debauching your finances? Debauching your human capital..."

Mark Steyn

title: When all men are paid for existing
grift: When all men are paid for existing
body:
## "When all men are paid for existing and no man must pay for his sins, then the Party is over."

G.K. Chesterton

title: When goods don't cross borders, soldiers will
grift: When goods don't cross borders, soldiers will
body:
## "When goods don't cross borders, soldiers will."

Frédéric Bastiat

title: You cannot strengthen the weak by weakening the strong
grift: You cannot strengthen the weak by weakening the strong
body:
## "You cannot strengthen the weak by weakening the strong."

Abraham Lincoln

title: You can't fix a problem until you identify it
grift: You can't fix a problem until you identify it
body:
## "You can't fix a problem until you identify it honestly. Today, there are tens of millions of Americans who have absolutely no idea that we are broke—broker than any society has ever been. Granted, America is not alone in its appetites; the G7 is essentially a group of addicts run by pathological liars."

Mark Steyn
